name: Obscurity
description: Obscuritydefines the visibility of creatures and objects based on environmental lighting and magical effects. A location is categorized as aClear Area,Lightly Obscured, orHeavily Obscureddepending on factors like ambient light, shadows, or spells which create areas such asFog,Darkness, andCloudkill. For example, in many cases the obscurity of an area can be increased – going from clear area to lightly obscured – by snuffing out a light source such as a candle. While a creature is simply standing in these areas, they are 'obscured' by the environment, but they only gain the specificLightly ObscuredorHeavily Obscuredstatus conditions if they successfully use theHideaction.

description: Adamage sourceis an attack or other effect that directly deals damage to one or more targets. Examples include striking a target with aweapon, manyspells, or thrown items such as anAlchemist's Fire. Damage sources usually require the use of anaction(e.g. throwing an object such as agrenade),bonus action(e.g. making an attack with your offhand weapon while dual wielding) orreaction(e.g. theHellish Rebukespell).

name: Servitor of the Black Hand Gloves
Armour
description: Armouris a type ofequipmentthat provides bonuses to the wearer'sArmour Class(AC), decreasing their chance of being hit by attacks.

name: Gnome
description: Gnomesare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Attacks
description: Attacksare attempts by acreatureat hitting a target, usually with aweaponor aspell.

name: Halfling
description: Halflingsare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Resting
description: Restingis a gameplay mechanic used to restore losthit pointsand depletedresources.

name: Dash
description: Dashing multiple times in a turn is possible with additional actions from features likeAction SurgeorHasteor by using bonus action dashes likeCunning Action: Dash. When dashing multiple times, the movement bonus fromDashstacksadditivelyrather than multiplicatively. For example, a character with 9 m (30 ft) movement speed who dashes twice gets 9 m (30 ft) bonus movement speed added twice for a total of 27 m (90 ft) (a 3x multiplier) rather than doubling the movement speed twice (which would be a 4x multiplier). Similarly, dashing three times applies a movement speed multiplier of 4x, not 8x.

name: Proficiency
description: Proficiencyis a representation of a creature's familiarity with something. When a creature has proficiency in something, they may add theirproficiency bonustodice rollsthat involve it.

name: Emerald Grove
description: The Emerald Grove occupies a vast part ofWildernessnorth of theNautiloid Wreckand east of theForest. It also is connected to theRisen Roadlocation. Entering this area for the first time grants the party 10XPat level 2, 30 XP at level 3, and 40 XP at level 4.

Nettie
description: Nettieis adwarvendruid in theCircle of the Emerald GroveinAct One. Nettie is a student underFirst Druid Halsin. In his absence, she is the primary healer in theEmerald Grove.

name: Invisible (Condition)
description: Generally, opening a chest to examine its contents does not break invisibility – adding or removing contents does.

name: Perception
description: Perceptionis aWisdom-basedSkill.

name: Underground Passage
description: The Underground Passage is a cavernous tunnel which runs roughly in parallel to the western side of theHollow, connecting certain parts of theEmerald Grovewith theForest. It is trapped with several guardian statues which hurl flames at any trespassers in sight that deal2d6⁠⁠Firedamage and inflict 3 turns ofBurningon a failedDC12DexteritySaving Throw.

description: Resistancesrepresent a creature's capacity to withstand certaintypes of damage.

name: Saving throws
description: Saving throwsare automatic dice rolls representing a creature’s attempt to “save” themselves from harm. They are provoked byspells, the hostile actions (other thanattacks) of other creatures, or hazards liketrapsandsurfaces. Saves are always associated with a specificability(except fordeath saving throws), and are always rolled against a targetDifficulty Class(DC), both determined by the triggering effect. When successful, they reduce or prevent harmful effects.

name: Abilities
description: Abilitiesare representations of a creature's physical and mental attributes inBaldur's Gate 3. There are six abilities inBaldur's Gate 3, and every creature has a set of sixability scores– numerical values which represent how well they perform at each ability.

name: Prone (Condition)
description: The following sources of Prone inflict the condition indefinitely. It can still be removed if the affected creature spends movement speed to get up. However, if the creature is unable to move (for example withEnsnared,Enwebbed,Frightened,Heavily Encumbered,Maimed, andRestrained), it remains prone indefinitely.

name: Spells
description: Spellsare magical effects that are created by creatures viaspellcasting.[1]Spells are frequently cast to dealdamageand providehealing, or to inflictconditionson their targets.

name: Hhune Mausoleum
description: The mausoleum can be accessed through the Lower City'sGraveyard, on the northern side beside the mortuary atX: 17 Y: 41. It requires eitherHhune Mausoleum Keywhich can be found in theKnights of the Shield Hideout, in a Mahogany Wardrobe atX: -744 Y: 537orDC 10Sleight of HandCheckto open the gate.

name: Lavernica's Home
description: Lavernica's Home is a single-storied small building located in the Steeps district, just outside the southeast entrance toBloomridge Park. It is a dwelling of the park groundkeeper Lavernica, who was charged with everyday tending to the park grounds.[1]

name: Stalker Svignee
description: Stalker Svignee is always hostile on sight. Defeating it allows the party to obtain a valuablePotion of Angelic Slumber.

name: Mind Flayer Colony
description: This area is accessed by jumping down into a tower after the fight againstKetheric Thormon theMoonrise Towers Rooftop. Upon arriving from the Moonrise Towers Rooftop, the party must destroy the membrane to move forward. The colony has many intellect devourers moving  around, which only become hostile if a fight breaks out nearby.

name: Last Light Inn
description: The Last Light Inn is a large guesthouse located in the northern part of theRuined Battlefield, in theShadow-Cursed Lands. It occupies a vast island between two waterstreams, which merge and run southward to flow into theRiver ChiontharnearMoonrise Towers.

name: Za'krug
description: When the party first arrive at the Emerald Grove, they findAradin,Barth, andRemiraattempting to enter the sanctuary. An arrow strikes the gatekeeper,Kanon, trapping the adventurers outside as a group ofgoblinsattack. Za'krug is the goblin boss who advances the ambush along with other goblins, abugbear, andworg. The group is immediately hostile to the party.

name: Western Beach
description: The beach is an unnamed location accessed by travelling west down the paths from both sides of theSouth Span Checkpoint, and then following along the shore of theRiver Chionthar. The steep slope descends from the grounds of the Gur Camp and the Sword Coast Couriers to the south from the checkpoint, and from the Open Hand Temple to the north of it.

name: Flymm Cargo
description: Flymm Cargo is a large warehouse on the eastern side of theGrey Harbour, detached from other riverport buildings. It occupies a patch of land adjacent to the end of theCounting House Docks, which is considered a restricted area. The northern side of the building is constantly in plain sight of aFlaming Fistpatrol. Thus, most ways to approach the entrances require using means of concealment orinvisibility. The dock berths around the warehouse, however, are not an off-limits area.

name: Roah Moonglow
description: Roah is an unscrupulous trader who has come to the goblin camp with the intention of buying goblin prisoners as slaves and selling Smokepowder. She is disappointed that the goblins are no longer willing to sell their prisoners, since they are now sending them toMoonrise Towers. She is more than willing to trade with the party, if they have coin.

name: Golbraith Stredivas
description: Golbraith is a retiredmind flayerhunter, and leading expert on mind flayers and counter-illithid strategies. He is currently dedicating his time to tracking those that have infiltrated the city, having passed the mantle and illithid-hunting responsibilities down to his son.

name: Kuo-toa
description: Kuo-toaare a non-playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3. They are degenerate and sadistic amphibian humanoids, but they can generally be communicated with.

name: Helm (God)
description: Helmis thegodof protection and guardians, lawful neutral. He is associated with the domainsLife,Light, Twilight.

name: Saving throws
description: Saving throwsare automatic dice rolls representing a creature’s attempt to “save” themselves from harm. They are provoked byspells, the hostile actions (other thanattacks) of other creatures, or hazards liketrapsandsurfaces. Saves are always associated with a specificability(except fordeath saving throws), and are always rolled against a targetDifficulty Class(DC), both determined by the triggering effect. When successful, they reduce or prevent harmful effects.

name: Shattered Sanctum
description: The Shattered Sanctum is the inner part of a former temple dedicated to⁠Selûne. It consists of a long entryway, a main nave which has side premises to the east and west, and the northern part, divided from the other areas and thus resembling a chancel. Beneath the northern part of the sanctum is a cave used by goblins as a spider pit.

name: Peartree House
description: Peartree House is a small private dwelling in the Eastway district of Baldur's Gate located close the huge outdoor staircase which leads from theBaldur's Mouthdown to theFelogyr's Fireworks. It has one floor and a basement.

name: Underdark
description: The Underdark in Baldur's Gate 3 is only a small portion of the true Underdark. TheDrow,DuergarandDeep Gnomesare native to the Underdark.

name: Flaming Fist
description: TheFlaming Fistis a mercenary company based inBaldur's Gate, where its members now serve as the city's police and military force.[1]

name: Sunlit Wetlands
description: The Sunlit Wetlands appear to be a peaceful and picturesque location south of theBlighted Villageand of theForest. It features a calm water stream, finding its way into a bigger river via a widespread, branched delta, and a smallRiverside Teahouse, whereAuntie Ethel, a trader from theHollow, is waiting to welcome even the unexpected guest.

name: Critical hit
description: Critical hitsoccur when an attackerrolls a natural 20on theirattack roll. Critical hits automatically land regardless of the target'sarmour class, and the attacker also rolls twice the normal number of dice to determine damage dealt.

name: Brand of the Absolute
description: TheBrand of the Absoluteis an optionalpermanent bonusthat leads to unique dialogue options and powerful item interactions.

name: Wyrm's Rock Fortress
description: The Wyrm's Rock Fortress is standing on a rocky isle in the middle of the river Chionthar, taking up the whole space save for minor cliffs around. Wyrm's Rock Fortress is connected to the south toWyrm's Crossing, and to the north to theLower Cityby two drawbridges. As the legend goes, the isle once was home to abronze dragon, who swore to defend the city from greatest dangers.

name: Ruined Battlefield
description: The Ruined Battlefield occupies approximately a half of theShadow-Cursed Lands, covering its eastern part and ending on the shore of the side stream running into the local course of theRiver Chionthar. To the north-west, it is adjacent to theLast Light Inn, a protected island of safety and currently a base of operations for theHarpers. Further to the west across the streambed lies theReithwin Town, another large part of the cursed area. To the south-west, at the shore of the major river, stands the main landmark of the whole cursed area: theMoonrise Towersstronghold, the destination point of the party's adventures inAct Two.

name: The Festering Cove
description: The Festering Cove is a separate large cavern in the Underdark located beneath the general level of the region. It features several waterfalls and rocky cliffs descending to the subterranean water stream, and is scattered with giant mushrooms and adorned with fishing nets and various shells and conches.

name: Adamantine Forge (location)
description: The Adamantine Forge is an area insideAbandoned Refuge, adjacent to theGrymforgearea, and shares the same region. From the area where the waypoint is located, a rocky path descends toX: -568 Y: 243, to the forge mechanism.

name: Fytz
description: Fytz the Firecrackeris a weapons trader who can be found in theLower CityinAct Three. She is also an old friend ofKarlach.

name: Upper City
description: TheUpper Cityis aregioninBaldur's GateinAct Three. It is the wealthy district of Baldur's Gate, that lays north of the city's oldest wall and its namesake gate. It is home to the city's noble patriar families, who typically live in estates. Contrary to the rest of Baldur's Gate where theFistoperates, the upper city has its own law enforcement calledThe Watch. The Watch are well-trained and authorised to use lethal force if patriars are threatened.[1]

name: Crèche Y'llek
description: The Githyanki who inhabit Crèche Y'llek conquered the area and slaughtered its previous inhabitants, a group of⁠Lathanderworshippers. It is used to launch operations along theRiver Chionthar, serving as a place to store supplies and loot, train new Githyanki warriors, and even hatch eggs. It also has a Planecaster and aZaith'isk.

name: Incontinentia Figgin
description: Incontinentia Figgin can be found dead in a cave behind the basement ofLavernica's Home.

name: Golbraith's Cellar
description: Golbraith's Cellar[1]is a small private house located directly across from the main gate toBloomridge Parkand north ofThe Blushing Mermaid. Though it seemingly consists of two adjacent dwellings, the door to the second one (with a separate mailbox) is blocked. The only entrance is atX: -154 Y: -55. The building is two-storied and has a spacious basement.

name: Cloister of Sombre Embrace
description: TheCloister of Sombre Embraceis the regional base of operations for the devout followers of⁠Shar, the goddess of darkness, void and loss. It was founded and developed byViconia DeVir, who is currently its leader, under the title of Mother Superior.

name: Emerald Grove
description: The Emerald Grove occupies a vast part ofWildernessnorth of theNautiloid Wreckand east of theForest. It also is connected to theRisen Roadlocation. Entering this area for the first time grants the party 10XPat level 2, 30 XP at level 3, and 40 XP at level 4.

name: Farlin
description: Farlinis a member ofThe Guildand envoy ofNine-Fingers Keene.

name: House of Healing
description: The House of Healing occupies the north-western corner of the town. It is a long two-storied building ending with a round hall; the second floor is only used above the round part, where the hospital library is kept. Along its northern and eastern side, a large graveyard outspreads, edging the riverbank. To the west is the morgue.

name: Wood Woad
description: Two Wood woads are found in theSunlit WetlandsduringAct One. They guard the secluded island of theDecrepit SanctuarywithAncient Mud Mephits.

name: Z'rell
description: Z'rell is first encountered in the Moonrise audience chamber withKetheric Thorm, where they are judging the failures of the Absolute's forces from theGoblin Camp. Ultimately, Ketheric Thorm delegates their punishment to the player character, and Z'rell orders them to report to her afterwards.

name: Rivington
description: Rivington is the southern-most suburb of Baldur's Gate, located on the southern shore of theRiver Chionthar, to the south ofWyrm's Crossing. Until recent events caused by the rising of theCult of the Absoluteit apparently was no more than a quiet village with several houses of wealthy citizens, and a large temple. Wide fields and hills around served as a place of temporary stay to various nomadic groups and peddlers.

name: Mountain Pass
description: The Mountain Pass location is a small area adjacent to theWaukeen's Restenvirons, actually being an extension of theRisen Road. The party can access it from the Risen Road approximately atX: -86 Y: 550, where several paths running from the inn and along the river shore collide. At the crossroads leading further along the Risen Road a wooden ramparts stands. The party can choose to walk under the rampart archway, cross it atop and descend from the other (western) side, or climb down the cragged rock to its east.

name: Rivington General
description: The Rivington General is a weapons and armour store located across theCircus of the Last Dayswith the main entrance atX: 3 Y: -31, to the north ofArfur's Mansion. The establishment actually consists of two buildings, connected by a large open-air forge on a high stone-paved dais, which accommodates the whole complex to the gradually raising level of the ground.

name: Stormshore Tabernacle
description: Stormshore Tabernacle is a shrine in the Eastway district of Baldur's Gate. It is not dedicated to a single god, but is a place where followers of most known deities of Faerûn can bring their devotion, prayers, and offerings, to be heard – and possibly blessed.

name: Grat
description: Grat can be found tending his makeshift shop in theGoblin Camp, which is located in the main area of the camp. He recently participated in a raid onWaukeen's Restand is trying to sell the items he collected from the dead. If the goblins in the courtyard are poisoned and on high alert, Grat will close his shop.

name: Arfur's Mansion
description: Arfur's Mansion is a large two-storied building atX: 11 Y: -95, across the main road immediately after entering Rivington for the first time. Otherwise, the mansion's entrance can be approached from both sides via side paths atX: 26 Y: -100andX: 1 Y: -77.

name: Reithwin Town
description: Reithwin Town occupies the western part of theShadow-Cursed Lands, divided both from theRuined Battlefieldand from theLast Light Innby side streams running south to flow into theRiver Chionthar. In its western end the Risen Road continues into the road toBaldur's Gate.

name: Redhammer
description: On approach, Redhammer pulls the party into a conversation and asks what they are doing. The party then has several options to respond:

name: Wyll
description: Wyll Ravengardis anOrigin Characterand a recruitableCompanion[1]who can beromancedby characters of any gender. He is aHumanand aFiendWarlockwith theFolk Herobackground. Wyll can be first found in theEmerald Grove(or atcampifKarlachwas recruited before him) duringAct One.

name: Goblin Camp
description: TheGoblin Campcovers a vast territory beyond the gulch of the river, which separates it from theBlighted Village. The gulch has several crossings allowing the party to enter the camp either openly or covertly.

name: Talli
description: Talli can be found inLast Light Inn'scourtyard during atX: -30 Y: 128, managing the Harpers' supplies.

name: The Guild
description: TheGuildis a criminal organization run by GuildmasterNine-Fingers Keene, which operates from withinBaldur's Gatecity.

name: Gauntlet of Shar
description: To progress through the dungeon, the party must solve a number of puzzles and encounters. However, the layout of the dungeon is fairly open-ended past the entry chamber and can be completed in a variety of ways, including entering it multiple times. No parts of the dungeon are restricted from fast travel and resting,except the Trials of Shar after entering the doors of those chambers. The dungeon has its own Waypoints (one past the entrance hall and another one before the descent into the Shadowfell).

name: Exxvikyap
description: Exxvikyap is the shop assistant of the blacksmith shop and an avid fan of its proprietor,Gyldro Angleiron.

name: Baldur's Gate
description: The city ofBaldur's Gateis a largeregionwithinAct ThreeofBaldur's Gate 3. It is the setting of nearly all of Act Three, and contains many individual locations. It can be seen on the map of theOver-world. It was founded byBalduranthe seafarer and is located on theSword Coaston the banks of theChionthar.

name: Ketheric Thorm
description: GeneralKetheric Thorm[5]is anUndeadwith elven ancestry and appearance,[4]the Chosen of⁠Myrkul, and one of the leaders of the enigmaticCult of the Absolute. He serves as one of the main antagonists inBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Gut
description: Priestess Gut is one of the spiritual leaders of the cult. She is one of three leaders at theShattered Sanctum, alongsideMintharaandDror Ragzlin.

name: Viconia DeVir
description: Viconia DeViris adrowclericof Shar. She is the Mother Superior of the⁠Sharenclave inBaldur's Gate. She is a returning character fromBaldur's GateandBaldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn, where she was a companion.[1]

name: Moonrise Towers
description: Moonrise Towers has always been the main seat of the Thorm family and its head,Ketheric Thorm. Under his lead, it has been a stronghold of⁠Selûne, then of⁠Shar, and (as of the game's events) as the base of operations for theCult of the Absolute. It is one of the few locations in the Shadow-Cursed Lands unaffected by theShadow Curse, the others beingLast Light Innand theSharran Sanctuary.

name: Reithwin Tollhouse
description: Reithwin Tollhouse occupies the south-eastern corner of the town adjacent to theRuined Battlefield. This two-storied building also has a large basement, which evens the descent of the ground level to the water end. Of all town buildings, this one looks most suffered from the shadow-cursed vines protruding from earth, partly rupturing and entangling its second floor.

name: Murder Tribunal
description: The Bhaalist Crypt's hall hosts members of a tribunal who can elect members of theCult of Bhaalto becomeUnholy Assassins of Bhaal.

name: Act Three
description: Act ThreeofBaldur's Gate 3is the final act of the game, taking place as the player arrives at the city ofBaldur's Gateafter leaving theShadow-Cursed Landsat the end ofAct Two.

name: Caïros
description: Caïrosis the envoy of theStone Lord, and can be found in the middle of an argument with theGuildon theWestern BeachinRivingtonduringAct Three.

name: Lower City
description: The Lower City has a number of interesting smaller locations.

name: Lyrthindor
description: Lyrthindor was a member ofKetheric Thorm'sDark Justiciar army, during the Sharran occupation ofReithwin Townand its surroundings over a hundred years ago. Originally a husband and father, the plague took his family and Lyrthindor turned to⁠Sharin his grief. He was one of many Sharrans who became a Justiciar through completion of the Gauntlet of Shar's trials, reaching the final test wherein he sacrificed the Nightsong.[1]

name: Grymforge
description: Built by worshippers of the goddess⁠Sharin time immemorial, this long-abandoned crumbling fortress has been recently given a semblance of new life once more. The duergar ofClan Flameshadehave been hired by the forces of theAbsoluteto seek an incursion point to theancient templedeeper below the fortress. Some members of the clan have also been swayed to the cause of the new power byKetheric Thorm'sretainer, the drowTrue Soul Nere. Others remained unconcerned and simply wish to bolster their armaments and thicken their purses. To reach both ends, the duergar are driving a group of enslavedIronhandgnomes, captured from theDecrepit Villageon the opposite shore of the Ebonlake.

name: Dammon
description: Dammon can be first met at his forge in theHollowin theEmerald Grove. He describes himself as a "bread-and-buttertinsmith" before the events of⁠Descent into Avernusand Elturel being pulled into the Hells.

name: Lann Tarv
description: Lann Tarvis a merchant on the ground floor ofMoonrise TowersinAct Two. His special inventory is not available unless passing certain checks after the introduction scene further within Moonrise Towers.

name: Sunlit Wetlands
description: The Sunlit Wetlands appear to be a peaceful and picturesque location south of theBlighted Villageand of theForest. It features a calm water stream, finding its way into a bigger river via a widespread, branched delta, and a smallRiverside Teahouse, whereAuntie Ethel, a trader from theHollow, is waiting to welcome even the unexpected guest.

name: Daughter of Darkness
description: Objectives and journal entries may vary pending story decisions.

name: The Hollow
description: The Hollow is a connective hub area adjacent to theEmerald Grove. It provides easy access to the other parts of the grove and harbours several local traders. Close before the game events commence it became a temporary haven to a group of refugees fromElturelled byZevlor, including several important characters.

name: Shadowfell
description: The Shadowfell is claimed as the domain of⁠Shar. Originally known as Plane of Shadow, Shar has bound negative energy to it and created the Shadowfell from it. Similar to theAstral Plane, gravity is weaker than on the material plane and it is easily possible to jump great distances through the shattered terrain.

description: Potion of Mind Reading has 1hit point.

name: Potion of Speed
description: Potion of Speed has 1hit point.

description: Antidote has 1hit point.

description: Alchemyis a crafting mechanic that allows to turn your collection of herbs, wild mushrooms, crystals, and monster components intopotions,elixirs,grenades, andcoatings. All alchemical ingredients including extracts of them are stored in theAlchemy Pouch.

name: Blinded (status group)
description: Blindedis astatus group. This group includes vision-impairing status similar toBlinded. While blinded by one of these conditions, manyreactionswill be unavailable such asOpportunity Attack,Hellish Rebuke, orDeflect Missiles. Creatures who do not rely on vision will generally be immune to all blinding conditions.

name: Broken Promises (Condition)
description: Although the tooltip states the reduction is applied "after Long Rest", any method of removing the debuff, such asRemove Curse,immediatelyactivates the strength reduction. Although the reduction is stated to be indefinite, it can be removed by dying and using aScroll of Revivify.

description: Dwarvesare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Potion of Feather Fall
description: Potion of Feather Fall has 1hit point.

name: Night Orchid
description: If first obtaining this item inAct Twoand the player character's approval withShadowheartis high enough, it can be given to her, prompting uniquecommentsand dialogue.

name: Abilities
description: Abilitiesare representations of a creature's physical and mental attributes inBaldur's Gate 3. There are six abilities inBaldur's Gate 3, and every creature has a set of sixability scores– numerical values which represent how well they perform at each ability.

name: Elixir of Barkskin
description: Elixir of Barkskin has 1hit point.

name: Half-Orc
description: Half-orcsare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3. They are born of bothhumanandorcancestry, and exhibit a blend of orcish and human characteristics, their appearances varying widely.

name: Tiefling
description: Tieflingsare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3. Touched by the Hells, tieflings are resistant to fire and carry extraordinary Infernal powers.

name: Resources
description: Resourcesare expendable units inBaldur's Gate 3that determine what acreatureis able to do at any given time, as well as how long they must wait before they are able to do it again.

name: Elixir of Fire Resistance
description: Elixir of Fire Resistance has 1hit point.

name: Dragonborn
description: Dragonbornare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3. They haveresistanceto a type of energy damage, and are able to make deadlybreath weapon attacks. These abilities are affected by their dragonborn subrace. Dragonborn resemble draconic humanoids, can have scales in a variety of colours, and may or may not have a tail.

name: Abilities
description: Abilitiesare representations of a creature's physical and mental attributes inBaldur's Gate 3. There are six abilities inBaldur's Gate 3, and every creature has a set of sixability scores– numerical values which represent how well they perform at each ability.

name: Enlarged (Condition)
description: TheDuergarversion ofEnlargeuses a unique icon and visual effect, but is functionally identical.

name: Elixir of Poison Resistance
description: Elixir of Poison Resistance has 1hit point.

name: Surprised (Condition)
description: Surprisedis a specialconditionthat can affectcreaturesduring the first round of combat.

name: Elixir of Darkvision
description: Elixir of Darkvision has 1hit point.

name: Haste Spores (Condition)
description: Does not stack withHastened.

name: Spectator
description: Spectators are a lesser type ofBeholder. They are floating creatures with a single huge eye and four eyestalks from which they may cast damaging or debilitating ray attacks. They come in two varieties, a normal version and a stronger variant with increased stats and HP.

name: Grease Bottle
description: Grease Bottle has 1hit point.

name: Noxious Fumes
description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Araj Oblodra
description: While Araj does at first feign the part of a studious researcher and alchemist, the grey-skinned, red-eyed drow has no qualms with deception, torture, murder, and even genocide.

name: Steel Watcher (creature)
description: Steel Watchersare NPCs mainly found in the city ofBaldur's Gate.

name: List of creature types
description: This page lists the various creature types found throughout Baldur's Gate 3.

name: Poison Cloud
description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Omeluum
description: Omeluumis a friendlyMind FlayerandtraderinBaldur's Gate 3. Omeluum comes to theEbonlake Grottoin theUnderdarkafter being contacted byBlurg. It is a member of theSociety of Brillianceand can be asked to help⁠remove their tadpole.

name: Oil
description: This area can be created by the following:

description: This area can be created by the following:

description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Alchemist's Fire
description: Alchemist's Fire has 1hit point.

name: Acid (Condition)
description: The Acid condition is removed after the target has been hit, if the target bleeds.

name: Fire (surface)
description: This area can be created by the following:

description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Dancing Lights (area)
description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Acid Vial
description: Acid Vial has 1hit point.

name: Bumpnagel
description: Bumpnagel can be found inAngleiron's CellarinRivingtonduringAct Three.

name: Helsik
description: Helsik is a warlock in service to the archdevil⁠Mammon, possibly the richest being in existence and a miser loyal only to coin. At the Devil's Fee, Helsik trades in curios and information, offering a large and rare collection of Hellish items accumulated through her occupation as a diabolist. However, Helsik was not satisfied with the coin she was bringing in from the curios, and decided to diversify her skills into providing "access".[1]

name: Scrying Eye
description: Scrying Eyes observe the actions of others and relay them to the secret leaders of theCult of the Absolute. They can report crimes and attempt to alert their allies, if they are attacked. Scrying Eyes can be found at outposts of the Absolute, including theShattered Sanctum,Grymforge, andMoonrise Towers.

name: Wyvern Toxin
description: Wyvern Toxin has 1hit point.

name: Drow Poison
description: Drow Poison has 1hit point.

name: Diluted Oil of Sharpness
description: Diluted Oil of Sharpness has 1hit point.

name: Wizardsbane Oil
description: Wizardsbane Oil has 1hit point.

name: Oil of Accuracy
description: Oil of Accuracy has 1hit point.

name: Simple Toxin
description: Simple Toxin has 1hit point.

name: Basic Poison
description: Basic Poison has 1hit point.

name: Arrow of Ilmater
description: Arrow of Ilmater has 1hit point.

name: Darkness (cloud)
description: This area can be created by the following:

name: Fiend
description: Fiendsare a type ofcreaturethat are from theLower Planes. They are creatures born of pure evil.

name: Monstrosity
description: Monstrositiesare a type ofcreaturefound within Baldur's Gate 3. They are unnatural creatures, more often than not considered to be the classical definition of a monster.

name: Dragon
description: Dragonsare a type of powerful and magicalcreaturethat exists in Baldur's Gate 3. There are many types of dragons, differentiated by their colour and shine of the scales on their skin.

name: Beast
description: Beastsare a type ofcreaturefound within Baldur's Gate 3. They are non-humanoid, but natural inhabitants of the world and come in many different shapes and sizes.

name: Arrow of Salving
description: Arrow of Salving has 1hit point.

name: Arrow of Roaring Thunder
description: Arrow of Roaring Thunder has 1hit point.

name: SG Paralyzed
description: SG_Paralyzedis astatus group.

name: Elemental
description: Elementalsare a type ofcreaturethat hail from the⁠Elemental planes. They are creatures that embody a single elemental plane.

name: Arrow of Ice
description: Arrow of Ice has 1hit point.

name: Arrow of Fire
description: Arrow of Fire has 1hit point.

name: Arrow of Transposition
description: Arrow of Transposition has 1hit point.

name: Arrow of Lightning
description: Arrow of Lightning has 1hit point.

name: Sorcerer Robe
description: These appear to be older models.[verify]

name: Let The Undead Blanch With Fear
Garb of the Land and Sky
Proficiency
description: Proficiencyis a representation of a creature's familiarity with something. When a creature has proficiency in something, they may add theirproficiency bonustodice rollsthat involve it.

name: Find the Missing Shipment
description: Find the Missing Shipmentis aQuestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by approaching the trappedRuganandOllyatthe Risen Road, finding theZhentarim Basementand speaking withZarysthere, or reading a note found on the corpse of one of her missing agents.

name: Heavy Chest
description: Heavy Chest has 100Hit Points.

name: Apothecary's Cellar
description: The Apothecary's Cellar is located underneath theBlighted Villageand is accessible from a hatch in the abandoned apothecary building, which is near the entrance of the village at coordinatesX: 32 Y: 379. Inside the building behind the main counter on the floor is a Wooden Hatch through which the party can descend into the cellar.

name: Baelen Bonecloak
description: Baelen andDerryth BonecloakrunBonecloak's Apothecaryin Baldur's Gate. Needing to restock one of the more popular and expensive items at their store,Noblestalk, they travelled to theUnderdark. The two set up a temporary camp inEbonlake Grottowith a myconid colony, and Derryth sent Baelen off to collect the mushroom.

name: Sunlit Wetlands
description: The Sunlit Wetlands appear to be a peaceful and picturesque location south of theBlighted Villageand of theForest. It features a calm water stream, finding its way into a bigger river via a widespread, branched delta, and a smallRiverside Teahouse, whereAuntie Ethel, a trader from theHollow, is waiting to welcome even the unexpected guest.

name: Whispering Depths
description: Whispering Depths can be accessed from three points, all of which are located in theBlighted Village.

name: Nere
description: Nere is trapped behind a cave-in near the main room inGrymforge. Several enslaveddeep gnomesare forced to work on freeing him by a group ofduergarslavers who are members of theCult of the Absolute. To save him, the gnomes tell the party to findPhilomeen, a runaway gnome in possession of an explosive capable of destroying the rubble.

name: Minotaur
description: Minotaurs are large humanoid creatures with bull heads and savage roars. They are known for being able to recall any path they have previously traveled. Minotaurs are native to theUnderdark. One of them attempts to ram through the entrance of the Selunite temple and another two can be found patrolling in the Underdark.

name: Sazza
description: When the party first approach theMakeshift Prisonthrough theEmerald Grovegateway, they find Sazza being held at the point of a crossbow byArka, who seeks revenge for her dead brotherKanon. Arka's friend,Memnos, attemps to dissuade her from her vengeance. A party member can choose to help Sazza by talking her down or stepping in the path of Arka's crossbow; they can also let Sazza die by Arka's hands. If Sazza is attacked after Arka and Memnos leave,[verify]she can call for a guard who then react as if any other neutral character was attacked.

name: Abdirak
description: Abdirak resides inside theShattered Sanctumnext to the torture room. He offers peace and serenity through pain. If the interacting party member accepts, Abdirak strikes them several times. If the player character isThe Dark Urgeorigin, unique dialogue options are also available, and Abdirak may show sympathy for their plight. Regardless, if he is pleased with their performance, he blesses them with thepermanent bonusLoviatar's Love, and the following inspiration:

name: Steal the Sacred Idol
description: Steal the Sacred Idolis aQuestin theDruid GroveinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be initiated by speaking withMolafter earning her trust by helping the various tiefling children in theDruid Grove.

name: Locke
description: Locke and his wifeKomiracan be found near the entrance of theSacred Pool, attempting to reach their daughterArabella, whomKaghahas taken prisoner after her failed attempt to steal theIdol of Silvanus. If the party interact with Locke and Komira, they can offer to speak with Kagha on their behalf. This initiates⁠Save Arabella.

name: Withers
description: Withers[3][4]is a camp companion who offers his various services to the party. Secretive, but wise and ever helpful, he can be found in theDank CryptinAct 1and remains with the party throughout the game. If asked to leave or who he is, he declines to do so.

name: Glut
description: Glut was the sovereign of a myconid circle, now displaced after duergar killed its people. Without a circle, Glut took shelter withSovereign Spaw'sin the Ebonlake Grotto. However, it holds resentment towards Spaw for not giving aid during the attack.

name: Mol
description: Molis atieflingrefugee inBaldur's Gate 3. She can be found at theEmerald Grovein theTiefling Hideout, and is the leader of the gang of refugee children hiding at the Grove.

name: Polma
description: Polma guardsPriestess Gut'schambers in theDefiled Temple. Convincing Polma to allow the party safe passage through the room requires aDC 22Ability Checkof eitherPersuasion,Intimidation, orDeception.

name: Hush You!
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Hush_You!_Action.mp4

name: Deliver Nere's Head
description: Deliver Nere's Headis aquestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started if the party talks toSovereign SpawatEbonlake Grotto, after completing⁠Defeat the Duergar Intruders.

name: Bernard
description: Bernardis theConstructguardian of theArcane Towerin theUnderdark. Created by the tower's owner,Lenore De Hurst, Bernard was designed for both protection and companionship.

name: Sceleritas Fel
description: When theDark Urgeis chosen asorigin, the player character suddenly finds out they have murderedAlfira(orQuil Grootslang, aDragonbornBard, if Alfira is unavailable). Whether they embrace this murder or not, Sceleritas Fel reveals that he is their humble butler,[2]giving small hints as to what kind of person the Dark Urge may have been. He actively encourages the Dark Urge to commit more deplorable acts of murder, claiming they should"Be true to yourself, my Lady/Lord."[3]He gifts the player character with theThe Deathstalker Mantleas a reward for murdering the Bard, and leaves them to their business.[4]

name: Sacred Munitions
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Sacred_Munitions_Action.mp4

name: Save Arabella
description: Save Arabellais aQuestavailable in theEmerald Grove, and begins when nearing theSacred Pool.Kagha, acting leader of the druids, is holding a tiefling child captive. Her parents want to make sure she is okay.

name: Voss
description: Kith'rak Vossis a ruthlessGithyankiknight in the service of his queen,⁠Vlaakith. He rides a red dragon namedQudenos.

name: Secret Chamber
description: The Secret Chamber is a hidden location within theCrèche Y'llek. It consists of a heavily trapped corridor and a large cavern with richly adorned pedestal combined with intricate machinery, and may remain hidden unless certain choices are made by the party.[1]

name: The Hollow
description: The Hollow is a connective hub area adjacent to theEmerald Grove. It provides easy access to the other parts of the grove and harbours several local traders. Close before the game events commence it became a temporary haven to a group of refugees fromElturelled byZevlor, including several important characters.

name: Ravaged Beach
description: The Ravaged Beach is the starting point ofAct One, where the player character wakes up after theNautiloidcrashes down. It is located on the bank of theRiver Chionthar; the area is dominated by bulky wreckage from the crash. The crashed ship is stretched from northwest to northeast; its giant tentacles are still pulsing, and the sand and rocks around littered with tissue splashes and hull shards.

name: Creature size
description: Acreature'ssizeindicates how large it is.

name: Kagha
description: Kaghais a wood elven druid in theEmerald GroveduringAct One. She was Halsin's Second Druid, but has named herself First Druid in his absence. She started theRite of Thornsto seal the Grove off, no matter the consequences.

name: Jhe'rezath
description: Jhe'rezath stands stoically alongside W'wargaz in the Inquisitor's Chamber ofCrèche Y'llek. She has little to say to the party, other than to brusquely encourage them to talk to the inquisitor instead.

name: Escape the Nautiloid
description: The first majorQuestofBaldur's Gate 3,Escape the Nautiloid, takes place immediately after the opening cinematic. The player character, now infected with amind flayertadpole, is aboard theNautiloidship when it is attacked bygithyankiwarriors. While trying to escape, the Mind Flayer controlling the ship warps the Nautiloid through different planes of existence before ending inAvernus, the first of theNine Hells. The objective of the Prologue is to find a way out of the Nautiloid, ideally by not dropping down into Hell.

name: Strange Ox
description: Walking past the Strange Ox prompts aDC 10InsightCheckto notice its odd behavior. A character who passes the check can talk to the Strange Ox viaSpeak with AnimalsorPotion of Animal Speakingand make aDC 10ArcanaCheckto investigate further:

name: Komira
description: Komirais one of the tiefling refugees seeking shelter in theEmerald GroveinAct One.

name: Pooldripp
description: Pooldripp is among other Kuo-toa inThe Festering Cove, worshiping the alleged Kuo-toa God of Murder,BOOOAL.

name: Gandrel
description: IfAstarionis in the party, Gandrel reveals he is hunting for Astarion, whom he believes is a vampire spawn that attacked their village and abducted a child. Otherwise, the party can learn this information by passing aDC 15PersuasionCheckorDC 20IntimidationCheck.

name: Flind
description: Flindis a hostilegnollwarlord inThe Risen Road Cavejust west ofThe Risen RoadWaypointduringAct One.

name: Hellsboar
description: Three hellsboars can be found in a small abandoned chapel in theGrymforge, along with aMerregon.Lesser Hellsboarscan be encountered in thePrologueon theNautiloid, andVengeful Boarscan be found in theHouse of Hope.

name: Escape the Nautiloid
description: The first majorQuestofBaldur's Gate 3,Escape the Nautiloid, takes place immediately after the opening cinematic. The player character, now infected with amind flayertadpole, is aboard theNautiloidship when it is attacked bygithyankiwarriors. While trying to escape, the Mind Flayer controlling the ship warps the Nautiloid through different planes of existence before ending inAvernus, the first of theNine Hells. The objective of the Prologue is to find a way out of the Nautiloid, ideally by not dropping down into Hell.

name: Rosymorn Monastery Trail
description: The description of the location provided here is given as if progressing in from the Mountain Pass. If entering the region from other possible points, the order of encounters might be shuffled.

name: Dread Hollow
description: The Dread Hollow occupies western and north-western parts of the Underdark region. The hollow forms a ring-like territory around a chasm, with a very large and brightly shining Sussur Tree growing in the middle[1]. To the north-east, a bunch of Knotted roots leads to a separate grotto overgrown with poisonous Bibberbang mushrooms.

name: Phase Spider Matriarch
description: ThePhase Spider Matriarchis a type ofMonstrositycreature inBaldur's Gate 3. It is a boss encounter in theWhispering Depthsand possesses several powerful abilities.

name: G'r'ath
description: Raider G'r'athis a member of the githyanki commune at Crèche Y’llek before the arrival ofInquisitor W'wargaz'sduringAct One.

name: Rath
description: Rath can be found in theInner Sanctumarguing withKagha, who is consideringArabella'spunishment after her failed attempt to steal theIdol of Silvanus. Rath is one of few who strongly disagrees with Kagha's leadership. He urges the party to findHalsin, reassuring them that he will keep an eye on Kagha in the meantime.

name: Finish the Masterwork Weapon
description: Finish the Masterwork Weaponis aQuestinAct OneofBaldur's Gate 3. It can be acquired by picking upHighcliff's Journalin one of the houses in theBlighted Villageor by lootingHighcliff's Blueprintsin the basement of the same house.

name: Thudd
description: Thudd is loyal to Clan Flameshade. If siding withBrithvar, he fights with the party againstNere.

name: Baretha
description: Baretha is part of a githyanki patrol searching theWildernessfor a powerful weapon. As thesarthin the group, she reports directly toKith'rak Voss. As the party approach Baretha and the githyanki patrol near the entrance to the Mountain Pass, they find a group ofFlaming Fistsdemanding they lay down their weapons. The Fists are swiftly defeated in a bath ofdragonflames.

name: Gnome
description: Gnomesare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Stornugoss
description: Ghustil Stornugossis the principal healer ofCrèche Y'llekduringAct One.

name: Avenge Glut's Circle
description: Avenge Glut's Circleis aQuestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by talking toGlutinEbonlake Grotto.

name: Selûnite Outpost
description: The Selûnite Outpost is located in the south-east corner of the Underdark region. It resembles a small fortress with ramparts and a portcullis. Behind it, scattered wooden barricades and long-dead skeletons of ancient fighters.

name: Blighted Village
description: The Blighted Village is a small location surrounded by theForest, through which the party can reach theGoblin Camp, theRisen Road, and theSunlit Wetlands, as well asWhispering Depths(and theUnderdarkthrough it). The buildings are half-ruined and neglected, though there are several points of interest to find and take several minor quests. Exploring the Blighted Village can progressShadowheart'spersonal quest, either asoriginorcompanion.

name: Alfira
description: Alfirais a tiefling bard inBaldur's Gate 3. She can be found at theEmerald Groveon a cliff overlooking the ritual, trying to compose a song.

name: Jeera
description: She is in Crèche Y'llek, south-east of its waypoint atX: 1387 Y: -821.[1][2]

name: Thulla
description: Thullais anIronhandgnome inEbonlake GrottoinAct One.

name: Waukeen's Rest
description: Waukeen's Rest is a two-story inn with a central building and two wings on either side. Neatly built in a natural rocky alcove, its premises make for a secluded pocket of space, divided from the outer world by a gate. Such a layout could provide strong defences – or turn into a deadly trap for those inside if caught off guard.

name: Ko'kuu
description: If speaking with Varsh Ko'kuu, the interacting party member can pass a passiveDC 10InsightCheckto deduce that Ko'kuu has been waiting a long time for the last egg in the hatchery to hatch. If the party member passes the Insight check, they can attempt aDC 10PersuasionCheckto ask Ko'kuu why hereallycares about this egg. If the party member failed the passive Insight roll, they can still ask Ko'kuu why he cares about one egg via aDC 14PersuasionCheck.

name: Auntie Ethel
description: Auntie Ethelis a fussy trader of potions, lotions, quirks, and sundries who can be first encountered inThe Hollowof theEmerald Grove. Later, she will return to her home at theRiverside Teahousein theSunlit Wetlands.

name: Merregon Legionnaire
description: One Merregon Legionnaire is in a locked room inGrymforge, accompanied by threeHellsboar. It attacks on sight. The door can be opened by passing aDC 18Sleight of HandCheckor using theTarnished Silver Key.

name: Fezzerk
description: Fezzerkand his gang can be found on the western most side of the Blighted Village where the windmill is, laughing atBarcus Wrootwho is strapped to the machine. This starts the quest⁠Rescue the Gnome. If approached, Fezzerk and his gang confront the party. The party can ask him what he is doing, or ask him to let them pass. If asking what he is doing, then the party can demand Barcus to be let down, either by persuading Fezzerk to pick another victim or intimidating him.

name: Zurk
description: Beastmaster Zurk can be found off to the side of the worg pens next to a table cutting upelfmeat to feed the captive Worgs.

name: Zhalk
description: Zhalk is encountered at the helm of theNautiloid, where he and his minions are fighting severalmind flayers. When the party enters the helm, combat immediately begins. Although it is not required to kill Zhalk to end the fight, he can be killed or disarmed for hisEverburn Bladeand extra experience.

name: Sunlit Wetlands
description: The Sunlit Wetlands appear to be a peaceful and picturesque location south of theBlighted Villageand of theForest. It features a calm water stream, finding its way into a bigger river via a widespread, branched delta, and a smallRiverside Teahouse, whereAuntie Ethel, a trader from theHollow, is waiting to welcome even the unexpected guest.

name: Overgrown Tunnel
description: The Overgrown Tunnel is an optional dungeon, accessible through the illusory fireplace in theRiverside Teahouse. The illusion is taken off byAuntie Ethelherself, if she is somehow angered by the party. Then she flees deeper into her lair. Otherwise, the false fireplace can be detected by aDC 10InvestigationCheck.

name: Dhourn
description: The battlefield west of the Selûnite Outpost is littered with petrified drow. Entering the area triggers the appearance of aSpectator, who is immediately hostile to the party. The spectator can unpetrify Dhourn and the other drow, who then fight alongside the creature, unless their trance is broken by taking damage.

name: Fire Bolt
description: ⁠Fire bolton theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Stool of Hill Giant Strength
description: TheStool of Hill Giant Strengthis an interactable item on the top floor of theArcane Tower, in the far corner under the thatched roof.

name: Gekh Coal
description: Gekh is hiding via invisibility inside theDecrepit Villagealong withGarmor,Holvik, andLurgan. If approaching from the high ground, then one of his hidden companions reveals themselves and tells Gekh that "someone is sneaking up on them". If approaching from the beach, then Gekh immediately addresses the party himself.

name: Xargrim
description: Xargrim's corpse is hidden in the vine-blocked section inEbonlake Grottonext toDerryth BonecloakatX: 55 Y: -90. The door is unlocked as a quest reward for⁠Defeat the Duergar Intruders. This area can also be reached viaMisty Step.

name: Ravaged Beach
description: The Ravaged Beach is the starting point ofAct One, where the player character wakes up after theNautiloidcrashes down. It is located on the bank of theRiver Chionthar; the area is dominated by bulky wreckage from the crash. The crashed ship is stretched from northwest to northeast; its giant tentacles are still pulsing, and the sand and rocks around littered with tissue splashes and hull shards.

name: Find the Missing Boots
description: Find the Missing Bootsis aQuestinAct OneofBaldur's Gate 3. It is initiated by speaking withGekh Coalat theDecrepit Villagein theUnderdark.

name: Worg Pens
description: Worg Pens serve both as a place where theGoblin Camp'sworgsare kept and also as prison for those party members who are arrested within the camp andShattered Sanctumterritory. The pens can be accessed from two points:

name: Ancient Sigil Circle
description: Ancient Sigil Circles(also known as 'Waypoints') are scattered throughout the world, and representBaldur's Gate 3'sfast travel system.

name: Yeva
description: Yevais ahumanfighterand a Gauntlet of theFlaming Fist. She can be encountered inAct OneandAct Twoof the game.

name: Forest
description: Hemmed in betweenEmerald Groveenvirons,Goblin Camp,The Risen Road, andSunlit Wetlands, this location actually encompasses theBlighted Villageand theOwlbear Cave, which are considered as separate locations. However, it also has several points of interest by itself.

name: Secluded Cove
description: The Secluded Cove can be accessed from theSacred Poolby heading the path to the north-east starting atX: 275 Y: 523. It is a vast area encompassing a series of cliffs connected by paths and patches of knotted roots, and several separately standing rocks as well.

name: Mimic
description: Mimics can transform to take on the appearance of ordinary objects, often resembling that of treasure chests. They lie in wait and grow long tentacles and sharp teeth, only revealing them just before feasting on unaware prey that get too close.

name: Ebonlake Grotto
description: TheEbonlake Grottooccupies the northern part of the Underdark and is a largeMyconidcolony. Several twisted alleys within it are side-lined by oversized mushrooms, with myconid guards and spore-animated corpses walking back and forth.

name: Owlbear Nest
description: There are two ways to enter the cave encompassing the Owlbear Nest.

name: Blurg
description: Blurgis a friendlyhobgoblintrader. AlongsideOmeluum, he is a member of theSociety of Brilliancestudying the flora and fauna of theUnderdark.

name: Volo
description: Volothamp Geddarm is a widely travelled wizard, most known for his guidebooks such as⁠Volo's Guide to Monstersand⁠Volo's Guide to Waterdeep.Elminster, an occasional fact checker and editor of his books - notably Volo'sGuide to Monsters- has said he is prone to "exaggeration, misrepresentation, and flights of fancy".[1]

name: Rescue the Grand Duke
description: Rescue the Grand Dukeis aquestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by approaching the burning inn onThe Risen Road,Waukeen's Rest.

name: Fiend
description: Fiendsare a type ofcreaturethat are from theLower Planes. They are creatures born of pure evil.

name: Inner Sanctum
description: The Inner Sanctum[1]is accessed through the stone door in theSacred PoolatX: 223 Y: 525. It can also be accessed from the hidden entrance through theUnderground Passage(see below).

name: Sacred Pool
description: The Sacred Pool is the central part of the Emerald Grove. It connects to theHollow, theSecluded Coveand theInner Sanctum. It also has direct access to the grove ramparts.

name: Save the Refugees
description: Save the Refugeesis aQuestinAct OneofBaldur's Gate 3with multiple related sub-quests. The party can encounter a group ofTiefling Refugeesat theEmerald Grovewho are trying to reach Baldur's Gate, but have been waylaid by goblin raiders.

name: Crusher
description: Crusher can be found in the eastern courtyard of the Goblin Camp, boasting to a small crowd about goblin superiority and the power of theAbsolute. If a non-drow player character interacts with him, he attempts to humiliate them and demands submission by kissing his foot. If the interacting party member is adroworhalf drow, Crusher does not attempt to bully them.

name: W'wargaz
description: A ruthless swordsman and psionic manipulator, W'wargaz can be considered thebossenemy ofCrèche Y'llek.

name: Karlach
description: Karlach Cliffgateis anOrigin Characterand a recruitableCompanion[1]who can beromancedby characters of any gender. She is aZariel Tiefling[2]and aBarbarianwith theOutlanderbackground.  Karlach can be first found on theRisen Roadduring Act One.

name: Tiefling Hideout
description: The Tiefling Hideout is a subterranean cavern hidden under the south-western part of theHollow. It is occupied byMoland her tiefling children gang,[1]who use it as a base to run scams and other exploits.

name: Rosymorn Monastery
description: The monastery can be approached only from theRosymorn Monastery Trail. The actual territory of the monastery starts near theRosymorn Monastery waypoint.

name: Elenna Thrinn
description: Thrinn is in the duergar encampment in Grymforge atX: -643 Y: 300, where she is organizing the rescue attempt to freeNerefrom the cave-in. She is in charge of the duergar in Nere's absence.

name: Gargantuan Cleave
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Gargantuan_Cleave_Action.mp4

name: Lava Elemental
description: These elemental creatures can manipulate lava. They rapidly heal while standing in lava and can use theirCreate Lavaability to create lava underneath them if no lava is nearby. Controlling and removing a Lava Elemental's access toLavais key to defeating one. Effects that prevent targets from regaining hit points, such as theBone Chillspell, prevent regeneration regardless of whether the elemental is in lava.

name: Act One
description: Act OneofBaldur's Gate 3begins immediately after the events of thePrologue, following theNautiloidcrash and theplayer characterwaking up on theRavaged Beach.

name: Filro
description: Filro is located near the Sussur Tree in theUnderdark. He is hostile toward the party from the start and fights alongside theHook Horrors, that patrol around the Sussur Tree. Filro holds theIcy Crystal, one of the three pieces required to rebuild the powerfulMourning Frost.

name: Watertight Chest
description: Watertight Chest has 50Hit Points.

name: Esther
description: Esther is an adventurer employed by theSociety of Brillianceto acquire agithyankiegg for a long-term nurturing experiment. Upon meeting Esther, she asks the party to steal an egg from the crèche insideRosymorn Monastery. The party can convince her to offer an advance payment by passing aDC 21PersuasionCheck, earning at least 200 gold with no effect on companion approval.

name: The Dark Urge
description: The Dark Urge[1]is an established character as well as aspecial origininBaldur's Gate 3. Suffering from amnesia, the Dark Urge remembers little but glimpses of their own past.

name: Dror Ragzlin
description: Dror Ragzlinis one of the three bosses at theGoblin Campwho works for theCult of the Absolute. The goblin bosses threaten theEmerald Groveandtiefling refugees.

name: Campsite
description: The campsite provides a location to swap or speak to party members, store or retrieve items from theTraveller's Chest, change class or revive fallenCompanionsviaWithers, or take aLong Rest. Through interacting with theMagic Mirror, Custom, Dark Urge, and Hireling characters (notOriginsorCompanions) can change their appearance. InMultiplayer, Withers' Wardrobe of Wayward Friends spawns near Withers when player characters are dismissed.

name: Anders
description: Anders,Cyrel,Trynn, and anunnameddeceased fourth companion are in theRisen Road Toll House.[1]If spoken to, Anders tells the party that the Toll House is safe under Tyr's protection. He explains that they are hunting a dangerous one-horned devil responsible for slaughtering refugees, who takes the form of aTiefling. He requests aid from the party in hunting the devil, initiating the quest⁠Hunt the Devil.

name: Kill Kagha
description: Kill Kaghais a sub-questof theDruid Grove'smain quest⁠Save the Refugees. It can be initiated by talking toZevlorafterKaghasends the party to assist him in getting the tieflings to safety.

name: Findal
description: Findalis a lightfoot halfling who can be found in theUnderground PassageinAct One.

name: Bibberbang
description: Bibberbang are highly flammable, and will also explode if a creature lingers close to a Bibberbang for too long. They are found primarily in theUnderdark, but some are also growing in the basement of theBonecloak's Apothecary.

name: Nautiloid
description: The player character starts on the Nautiloid immediately after character creation and must escape from it before the ship crashes inAvernus. Unlike most areas in-game, the Nautiloid has a linear layout where the party can only proceed in one direction.

name: Investigate Kagha
description: Investigate Kaghais asub-questof theEmerald Grove'smain quest,⁠Save the Refugees. It can be initiated by reading theRite of Thorns, found in the Emerald Grove, or finding another clue hinting towardsKagha'sscheme.

name: Overgrown Ruins
description: The ruins consist of the exteriorChapel Entranceand the interiorRefectoryandDank Crypt.[1]

name: Guardian of Faith (creature)
description: Guardians of Faith can be summoned with thelevel 4 Conjuration spell,Guardian of Faith.

name: Florrick
description: Florrick is professional and dutiful in her role as a leading member of Baldur's Gate's government. While an advisor, she prefers to be direct and on-hand when it comes to handling problems which plague the city, such as the disappearance of Ulder Ravengard. Being straightforward and honest in her dealings makes her quite a rarity in the oft corrupt ranks of the city's elite.

name: Kobold Looter
description: Kobolds normally band together in large groups, using their varying skills and numbers to take down far tougher foes. Kobold looters are mainly found in theRosymorn MonasteryofAct One.

name: Help Omeluum Investigate the Parasite
description: Help Omeluum Investigate the Parasiteis a sub-questofAct One'smain quest,⁠Find a Cure. It can be initiated by speaking withBlurgandOmeluumatEbonlake Grotto(the Myconid Colony), and informing them of the parasite.

name: Corrosive Strike
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Corrosive_Strike_Action.mp4

name: Arcane Tower
description: The Arcane Tower is in the south-west corner of the Underdark map. This area is recognizable by its neatly built stone entryway that opens to a front garden featuring twoArcane Turrets. It can be reached through the Torchstalk Gulch, recognisable by its dark brown paths paved with deep red mushroom clusters and spots of Torchstalk that cast floating embers into the air.[1]

name: Moss-Covered Chest
description: Moss-Covered Chest has 50Hit Points.

name: Far'aag
description: Far'aag is initially friendly to the party, but can become hostile depending on dialogue choices taken during dialogue.

name: Find the Mushroom Picker
description: Find the Mushroom Pickeris aquestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by talking toDerryth BonecloakinEbonlake Grotto.

name: Derryth Bonecloak
description: Derryth Bonecloakis agold dwarfandtraderinEbonlake Grotto.

name: Hunt the Devil
description: Hunt the Devilis aquestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3.

name: Arron
description: Arron is the first merchant the party can meet and trade with inBaldur's Gate 3. He sells some "bits and bobs" that he no longer needs.

name: Myconid
description: Myconidsare a non-playableraceof ambulatory and sentient fungi inBaldur's Gate 3.

name: The Risen Road
description: The Risen Road is located to the north of theBlighted Village. It can be accessed from several points:

name: Defeat the Duergar Intruders
description: Defeat the Duergar Intrudersis aQuestinAct Oneof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be initiated by approaching the Myconid Colony atEbonlake Grottopeacefully, and speaking withSovereign Spaw.

name: Adamantine Forge (disambiguation)
description: Adamantine Forgemay refer to:

name: Abandoned Refuge
description: The terrain of the Abandoned Refuge consists of crumbling architecture interspersed with caverns and lava flows, which frequently require jumping, or similar measures, to navigate. In this area, the player may discover two Mithral veins, from which they can extractMithral Ore. This can be used in the adjoining theAdamantine Forgelocation.

name: Ancient Sigil Circle
description: Ancient Sigil Circles(also known as 'Waypoints') are scattered throughout the world, and representBaldur's Gate 3'sfast travel system.

name: Decrepit Village
description: The Decrepit Village occupies the central area of the Underdark, gradually descending from the average level of the region to the sandy beach of the Ebonlake. It visually consists of two levels, with ramshackle wooden structures on the upper one, and rope nettings leading down to the shore with two berths.

name: Tara
description: Tarais a⁠Tressym, a cat-likeMonstrosity, andGale'sfluffy companion and assistant.

name: Brem
description: Bremis atraderin theZhentarim BasementinAct One.

name: Defiled Temple
description: The Defiled Temple is a secluded part of theShattered Sanctumof the former⁠Selûnitetemple. Since goblins have turned the temple into their camp, the Defiled Temple serves asPriestess Gut'spersonal chambers. It can be accessed through several points:

name: Therezzyn
description: Kith'rak Therezzynis the commander ofCrèche Y'llekinAct One.

name: Save the Goblin Sazza
description: Save the Goblin Sazzais aQuestinAct One. It is initiated by entering theEmerald Groveprison and intervening in the confrontation betweenArkaandSazza.

name: Inner Sanctum
description: The Inner Sanctum[1]is accessed through the stone door in theSacred PoolatX: 223 Y: 525. It can also be accessed from the hidden entrance through theUnderground Passage(see below).

name: Mizora
description: Best described as a sadistic[2]infernal lawyer, Mizora is one ofZariel'smost important allies. As Wyll's patron, she is directly related to his fate throughout the game, and the party has to deal with her whether they like it or not. Initially kept a secret by Wyll due to pact clauses, Mizora is the source of his powers and the reason why he was cast out from Baldur's Gate following young Ravengard's massacre ofTiamatcultists. Ever since, she has kept Wyll on a leash, using him for her own wicked interests.

name: Spaw
description: Sovereign Spaw's colony was attacked byduergarslavers who were trying to recaptureThulla, a runawaydeep gnomeslave the Myconids were giving safe harbour to. They were able to repel the deep dwarves, but sought help to finish off the invaders.

name: Plant
description: Plantsare a type ofcreaturethat are plant-like or fungal in nature. They can be found throughout Baldur's Gate 3, most notably in the Underdark in Act One, or in shadow-cursed form during Act Two.

name: BOOOAL
description: The party can realize first that BOOOAL is not a real god, but actually a common redcap using various illusion spells to deceivePooldrip the Zealous, who leads the kuo-toa. This allows the party to start poking holes in his story.

name: Zevlor
description: Zevlor was a Commander of theHellridersofElturel. After the Fall of Elturel, many people started viewing tieflings with distrust thinking their race were to blame for it. Due to this, Zevlor was forced to leave his position and abandon the city, taking a group of tieflings with him in the hopes for a better life atBaldur's Gate.

name: Giant Eagle
description: Giant Eagles are not overly aggressive creatures, yet highly territorial and will attack in order to defend their nests. They can be spoken to usingSpeak with Animals.

name: Umr'a'ac
description: Gish Umr'a'ac can be initially found in theCrèche Y'llekInfirmary, near the bunk beds, pondering over a book. He viciously derides anyone infected with aghaiktadpole, if spoken to as a non-gith; however, he reveals a softer side to gith characters, expressing concern over a colleague who went missing after using theZaith'isk.

name: Rescue Volo
description: Rescue Volois aQuestin theGoblin CampduringAct OneofBaldur's Gate 3. It is initiated as soon as the party enter the area and engage withVolothamp Geddarm, who has been captured and is forced to perform onstage.

name: Gale
description: Gale Dekariosis anOrigin Characterand recruitableCompanionwho can beromancedby characters of any gender. He is ahumanwizardwith theSagebackground. Gale can be first found at theRoadside CliffswaypointduringAct One.

name: Ebonlake Grotto
description: TheEbonlake Grottooccupies the northern part of the Underdark and is a largeMyconidcolony. Several twisted alleys within it are side-lined by oversized mushrooms, with myconid guards and spore-animated corpses walking back and forth.

name: Lump
description: Lump is in the Blighted Village withChockandFank. If approaching Lump, he asks to see theirBrand of the Absolute. If the brand has not been received, Lump attacks unless he is convinced otherwise:

name: Ormn
description: Ormn can be found down the hill from the elevator in theEmerald Grove. He is one ofHalsin'sanimal companions. Ormn will continuously throw fish onto the ground if the party pick them up, meaning he can supply an infinite amount of camp supplies.

name: Mystra's Blessing (Staff of Arcane Blessing) (Condition)
description: The in-game description of this condition is incorrect, and reads as follows:

name: Zhentarim Basement
description: TheZhentarimBasement is a large subterranean cave beneath theWaukeen's Restinn. It features deep basins spanned by stone arcs and bridges. Various levels of the paths are connected with wooden ladders and footways. In its far end is a descend to theUnderdark.

name: Minthara
description: Ruthless in service of theAbsolute, she commands a camp ofgoblinsplanning an attack on theEmerald Grovein the name of their god. Initially hostile, she can be re-encountered duringAct Twoand saved from execution.

name: Gnoll
description: Gnollsare non-playablehumanoidcreatures inBaldur's Gate 3. They closely resemblehyenasand are consumed with a primal bloodlust and hunger. Oftentimes, gnolls worship the demon lordYeenoghu.

name: Bulette
description: Bulettes are extremely dangerous monstrosities. Their natural armour consists of thick plates layered across their bodies. They are typically native to hill environments but can be found in many different locations. Bulettes are sometimes referred to as⁠Landsharks.

name: Origins
description: Originsare player avatars inBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Brithvar
description:
Brithvar can be encountered discussing with his lieutenantKurat the duergar encampment inGrymforgeatX: -624 Y: 355. 
Like many duergar at Grymforge, Brithvar holds no loyalty towards the Absolute and resents Nere for withholding their promised payment. While initially suspicious of the party, as his ability as a duergar to detectIllithidpresences makes him believe them to be aTrue Soul, he can be convinced otherwise and talked into leading a mutiny against Nere through advancing⁠Blind the Absolute, causing half the duergar to fight against Nere once thedrowis freed. After leaving the area or taking a long rest, he and the surviving duergar leave Grymforge beforeAbsolutereinforcements arrive. He can be persuaded or intimidated into leaving the enslaved deep gnomes behind, allowing them to return toBaldur's Gate.[1]

name: Gnome
description: Gnomesare a playableraceinBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Weapons
description: Weaponsare a type ofequipmentused inattacksthat deal damage. Weapons also giveproficientwielders access to specialweapon actions, and are an essential tool for any adventurer.

name: Vanessa
description: Sister Vanessa is one of the surgical assistants ofMalus Thormin theHouse of Healing. She and several other undead nurses are learning to grasp the science provided by Thorm on a strugglingDistressed Patient.

name: Mattis
description: Mischievous but good-natured, Mattis works as a scam artist forMol'schild-gang. His younger sister isSilfy.

name: Anya
description: Sister Anya is one of theZealous AssistantsofMalus Thormin theHouse of Healing. She and several other undead nurses are trying to grasp the science provided by Thorm on a strugglingDistressed Patient.

name: Willinx Jindlebee
description: Willinx is always hostile when encountered. Along with her allied necromancersDuckie VerishandSarabayle, she flanks the army of undead that greet anyone that trespass into the prison.

name: Marcus
description: Marcuscan refer to:

name: The Warden
description: The Warden can be spoken to at her post inMoonrise Towers Prison. She alternates between remaining inside her room, patrolling to the ominous fleshy hole in the ground, or speaking toWulbren.

name: Balthazar
description: Balthazaris an Undead necromancer, who can first be encountered in theGauntlet of Sharduring Act Two.

name: Blinding Shot
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Blinding_Shot_Action.mp4

name: Rescue the Tieflings
description: Rescue the Tieflingsis aquestinAct Twoof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking with theTieflingsat theLast Light Inn.

name: Camp followers
description: Camp followersare characters that travel with the player’s party and often remain at camp.

name: Eldritch Knight
description: Eldritch Knightis one of theSubclassesofFighter. These Fighters specialize inspellcasting, using their martial prowess and magic to attack enemies or support allies in combat. Eldritch Knights useIntelligenceas theirSpellcasting Ability, and learn spells from theWizard spell list, favouring the schools ofEvocationandAbjuration.

name: The Waning Moon
description: The Waning Moon is located westward from the central square. It is a distillery and a bar, clearly the den of leisure and pleasure for the Reithwin citizens before theShadow Cursefell. It is a huge building adjacent to a rocky ridge enframing the northern part of the city. Exploring the Waning Moon is key to progressing the quest⁠Punish the Wicked, which can be started at theRuined Battlefield.

name: Yurgir
description: Yurgir is an orthon who has been trapped in the Gauntlet of Shar for many years, unable to fulfil the requirements of his contract with Raphael. Under the devil's orders, he and his merregons devastated Ketheric Thorm's Dark Justiciars, killing all they found from the Grymforge to the Gauntlet.

name: Radija
description: Radija can be encountered outside the ornate door leading tothe rooftop. IfKetheric Thormis already on the roof, the party can ask for an audience with him, which she refuses.

name: Shadow-Cursed Vine Blight
description: Shadow-Cursed Vine Blights, like its brethren theShadow-Cursed Shambling Moundand theShadow-Cursed Needle Blight, conceal themselves within the surrounding vegetation in order to ambush passersby. The party must pass aDC 30PerceptionCheckto spot these ambush parties and avoid being surprised.

name: The Blade of Frontiers
description: The Blade of Frontiersis theCompanionQuestforWyll. It is automatically added to the journal when he joins the party inside theDruid Grove.

name: Isobel
description: Isobel is a cleric of Selûne who is helping a group ofHarperswho are stationed at the Last Light Inn. She has been using her Selûnite magic to maintain the moon shield that is protecting the inn from the Shadow Curse. Without her protection, the inhabitants of the inn would succumb to theShadow Curse.

name: Aylin
description: Dame Aylinis anAasimarand daughter of⁠Selûnewho appears inBaldur's Gate 3. She is first introduced duringAct Two, and her story plays a key role in the mysteries surrounding theShadow-Cursed LandsandMoonrise Towers. She can become aCamp Followerand later anAttached Follower.

name: Isobel
description: Isobel is a cleric of Selûne who is helping a group ofHarperswho are stationed at the Last Light Inn. She has been using her Selûnite magic to maintain the moon shield that is protecting the inn from the Shadow Curse. Without her protection, the inhabitants of the inn would succumb to theShadow Curse.

name: Geanne
description: Sister Geanne is one of theZealous AssistantsofMalus Thormin theHouse of Healing. She and several other undead nurses are learning to grasp the science provided by Thorm on a strugglingDistressed Patient.

name: Shadow-Cursed Needle Blight
description: Needle Blights, like its brethren theShadow-Cursed Vine Blightand theShadow-Cursed Shambling Mound, conceal themselves within the surrounding vegetation in order to ambush passersby. The party must pass aDC 30PerceptionCheckto spot these ambush parties and avoid being surprised.

name: Shadow Mastiff Alpha
description: This monstrous dog, composed of shadow and sinew, is always hostile. It appears out of the shadows alongside threeShadow Mastiffsin theRuined Battlefield, nearX: -49 Y: 36, when the nearbyever-burning torchesare destroyed.

name: Sharran Sanctuary
description: The Sharran Sanctuary is a hidden subterranean location in the middle of Reithwin Town inShadow-Cursed Lands. Unlike theGauntlet of Shar, located under theGrand Mausoleum, it is only a small fane dedicated to⁠Sharwith several effigies of the goddess, through which she can bestow her benevolence - or vent her ire - onto those who appeal to her.

name: Kansif
description: Kansif is initially found in the outskirts of theRuined Battlefield, nearby the entrance from theRosymorn Monastery Trail. He can be found a short jog away from the entry point, idly waiting for the arrival of aTrue Soul, and leading a small group ofAbsolutistscomposed of fellow half-orcBedi, and the goblinsBrawler Vez,Warrior Gronag, andSharp-Eye Lora.

name: Moonrise Towers Prison
description: The prison can be found beneathMoonrise Towersin theShadow-Cursed Lands.

name: Shadow-Cursed Shambling Mound
description: Shadow-Cursed Shambling Mound, like theShadow-Cursed Vine Blightand theShadow-Cursed Needle Blight, conceals itself within the surrounding vegetation in order to ambush passersby. The party must pass aDC 30PerceptionCheckto spot it and avoid being surprised.

name: Duckie Verish
description: Duckie is always hostile when encountered. Along with her allied necromancersWillinx JindlebeeandSarabayle, she flanks the army of undead that greet anyone that trespass into the prison.

name: Gauntlet of Shar
description: To progress through the dungeon, the party must solve a number of puzzles and encounters. However, the layout of the dungeon is fairly open-ended past the entry chamber and can be completed in a variety of ways, including entering it multiple times. No parts of the dungeon are restricted from fast travel and resting,except the Trials of Shar after entering the doors of those chambers. The dungeon has its own Waypoints (one past the entrance hall and another one before the descent into the Shadowfell).

name: Punish the Wicked
description: Punish the Wickedis aquestinAct Twoof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withHe Who Wasin theRuined Battlefield.

name: Oliver
