description: Olivercan be encountered duringAct Two. He can typically be found in theHouse in Deep Shadowslocation, within theShadow-Cursed Landsregion.

name: Arabella
description: Arabellais a tiefling child in theEmerald GroveduringAct One. She is the daughter ofLockeandKomira.

name: He Who Was
description: He Who Wasis aShadar-kaifound in theRuined BattlefieldatX: 129 Y: 106who gives the questPunish the Wicked. He has aRavencompanion.

name: House in Deep Shadows
description: The farmhouse is found south-east ofLast Light Inn, just up the road east of the Shadowed Battlefield waypoint. This location is also marked as theHouse of Flowersduring the quest⁠Lift the Shadow Curse, and is the area thatHalsinis referring to when he asks the party to seek "a place where flowers still grow".

name: Shadow-Cursed Lands
description: EncompassingReithwin Townand its surroundings, the area is almost entirely shrouded in the namesakeShadow Curse. Creatures without protection suffer the effects of theShadow Curse, and do not last long before dying or becomingShadow-Cursed Undead.

name: Act Two
description: Act TwoofBaldur's Gate 3takes place afterAct One, as the player enters theShadow-Cursed Landsto reachMoonrise Towersand find the heart of theCult of the Absolute.

name: Tska'an
description: Ch'r'ai Tska'anis agithyankiwarlock[1]encountered inAct Two.

name: Mig
description: Migis anogreworking for theCult of the AbsoluteinMoonrise Towers. She loves meat and claims to hear "meat-things" moving in the walls.

name: Grand Mausoleum
description: An ancestral burial place for the Thorm family, is located in the far end of the graveyard. It is actually a large cavernous area inside the rock, separated from the world with a high cast-iron gate.

name: Gauntlet of Shar
description: To progress through the dungeon, the party must solve a number of puzzles and encounters. However, the layout of the dungeon is fairly open-ended past the entry chamber and can be completed in a variety of ways, including entering it multiple times. No parts of the dungeon are restricted from fast travel and resting,except the Trials of Shar after entering the doors of those chambers. The dungeon has its own Waypoints (one past the entrance hall and another one before the descent into the Shadowfell).

name: Moonrise Towers Rooftop
description: The way to the rooftop is past the Ornate Door on the first floor, guarded byRadija,Necromites(4x) and, possibly,Glourik.

name: Kressa Bonedaughter
description: Kressa is aMyrkulitenecromancer[1]working underBalthazar, performing research and experiments on tadpolled victims in the Mind Flayer Colony. When met, she is rallying her fellow cultists,Hairy Henry,IronfingerandMaghtew Budj. She provides theMyrkul's Giftbuff.

name: Marcus Falgor
description: Upon reachingLast Light Inn,Jaheirais instantly wary of the party and summons vines to stop the player character. She believes the party areTrue Souls. If Mol does not surviveAct One, Marcus comes to vouch for the party, telepathically revealing that he is an Absolutist spy.

name: Barcus Wroot
description:
Barcus Wroot is a deep gnome who lived in the Underdark with his good friend, Wulbren Bongle. Wulbren and Barcus eventually left, Wulbren to places unknown and Barcus to the city ofBaldur's Gate. While in the city, Barcus found a bloodied amulet that he gave Wulbren before he left, compelling him to return to the Underdark in search of Wulbren. 
After rescuing Wulbren, the party can also learn that Barcus has very different ideas about how the Ironhand Gnomes should be handling Wulbren's rediscovery of the long lost formula to makeRunepowder, a fabled explosive of tremendous power.

name: Necrotic Laboratory
description: Upon entering the area, a battle with a large number of undead commences, including: oneDeath Shepherd(lv.6), fourWinged Horrors(lv.3), fourGreater Zombies(lv.7), and sixZombies(lv.3). During the battle, the Death Shepherd castsNo Rest for the Wicked, rising twoReconstituted Duellists, aReconstituted Marksmanand aReconstituted Arcanist(one of those per round).

name: Find Arabella's Parents
description: Find Arabella's Parentsis aQuestinAct Twoof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withArabellainReithwin Town.

name: Breath Attack
description: Breath attacks(also known asbreath weapon attacks) are short-range attack actions available only toDragonborn.

name: Kar'niss
description: Kar'nissis adriderin service of theAbsolute, whom he serves after being cast out byLolth, the Queen of Spiders.

name: Find the Nightsong
description: Find the Nightsongis aquestinAct One,Act Two, andAct ThreeofBaldur's Gate 3. It can be initiated by speaking withAradin Benoabout the reason for his expedition into goblin territory, or coming across the corpses of the failed expedition. This quest is only completable in Act Three.[1]

name: Thisobald Thorm
description: Thisobald Thormis a unique, intelligent Undead, and the master brewer ofThe Waning Moon. He is possibly the son ofKetheric Thorm.

name: Mason's Guild
description: The Mason's Guild with its extensive yards takes up the north-eastern part of Reithwin Town. It is a formidable building itself, and the stoneworks all around the city are evidence of the craftmanship of its past members. Both yards of the guild are cluttered by untouched blocks of stone, as well as half-chiselled statues and plaques.[1]It is separated from the town main by several gates, and its backyard opens onto the bank of the sidestream of theChionthar. Across it, the shining dome of the protectedLast Light Inncan clearly be seen.

name: Malus Thorm
description: Malusis the undead master surgeon of theHouse of Healing, twisted follower of⁠Shar, and member of the Thorm family.[2]

name: Susdera
description: Susderais a necromancer[1]of theCult of Myrkul. She is found atopMoonrise Towers, and assistsKetheric Thormin battle against the party.

name: Campsite
description: The campsite provides a location to swap or speak to party members, store or retrieve items from theTraveller's Chest, change class or revive fallenCompanionsviaWithers, or take aLong Rest. Through interacting with theMagic Mirror, Custom, Dark Urge, and Hireling characters (notOriginsorCompanions) can change their appearance. InMultiplayer, Withers' Wardrobe of Wayward Friends spawns near Withers when player characters are dismissed.

name: Gerringothe Thorm
description: Gerringothe Thormis a unique intelligent Undead, the toll-master of theReithwin Tollhouse.

name: Sarabayle
description: Sarabayle is always hostile when encountered. Along with her allied necromancersWillinx JindlebeeandDuckie Verish, she flanks the army of undead that greet anyone who trespasses into the prison.

name: Shadowheart
description: Shadowheart(born Jenevelle[2]Hallowleaf) is anOrigin Characterand a recruitableCompanionwho can beromancedby characters of any gender. She is aHigh Half-Elfand aTrickery DomainClericwith theAcolytebackground. Shadowheart can be first found on theNautiloidduring the prologue where she serves as a temporary companion, and can be properly recruited at various points inAct One.

name: Talli
description: Talli can be found inLast Light Inn'scourtyard during atX: -30 Y: 128, managing the Harpers' supplies.

name: Cursed Kuo-Toa Chief
description: Cursed Kuo-Toa Chiefis aKuo-toacreature located inAct Two. It is found in the abandoned fishing shack north of theHouse of Healing.

name: Hunna
description: Sister Hunna is one of theZealous AssistantsofMalus Thormin theHouse of Healing. She and several other undead nurses are learning to grasp the science provided by Thorm on a strugglingDistressed Patient.

name: Abilities
description: Abilitiesare representations of a creature's physical and mental attributes inBaldur's Gate 3. There are six abilities inBaldur's Gate 3, and every creature has a set of sixability scores– numerical values which represent how well they perform at each ability.

name: Wyrm's Crossing
description: Wyrm's Crossing occupies the wide stone bridge that connectsRivingtonand theWyrm's Rock Fortress. It resembles an urban area much more than Rivington, and consists of several establishments of trade and entertainment, built up on both sides of the bridge and forming its borders.

name: Borri Paver
description: Borri is located at in Foundry Labs of the Steel Watch Foundry atX: -1939 Y: 442. He carries aMotivatorwhich he can activate in combat.

name: Tamia Holzt
description: Tamia Holzt guards theSteel Watch Foundryand watches over the Gondians which are forced to work there to produce theSteel WatchersforEnver Gortash.

name: Carm's Garms
description: Carm's Garms[1]is a two-storied building located in the north-east ofWyrm's Crossing, across fromDanthelon's Dancing Axe. It has no cellar, featuring a subjacent tunnel instead, accessible from the outside wooden sidewalks of the Crossing bridge. Near the store building, three machinery enthusiasts -Trinigan Gazotts,Slozy ArminiferandVinegaroon- discuss spiritedly the technical features of the Steel Watchers.

name: South Span Checkpoint
description: TheSouth Span Checkpointis accessed by following the main road which divides Rivington in two uneven parts. The checkpoint serves as the main access point to theWyrm's Crossing, through which the party can reach theWyrm's Rock Fortressand theLower Cityspreading behind it.

name: Aelis Siryasius
description: Aelis Siryasius inhabits an area surrounded byGrease Elementals,Grease Mephits, andGreaseballs. Upon approaching Aelis' grease summons, he warns the party to halt and accuses them of working with theCult of the Absolute. Over the course of a rambling speech, he reveals he was one of the "three finest thieves in Baldur's Gate" alongsideBarekiandSarinbefore the trio broke intoEnver Gortash'soffice. In doing so, they discovered the secret plot ofThe Absoluteand were hunted by Absolutists until they fled into the sewers. They became increasingly paranoid until Sarin believed that Aelis and Bareki "had gone strange" and hid the treasure they stole from Gortash. Convinced she was taken over by the Absolute, Bareki decapitated her and Aelis burned her until only her skeleton remained. Aelis' account of what happened to Sarin hints that Bareki hasSarin's Skull.

name: Popper
description: Popper has theClown's Severed Handon display in his shop. If caught stealing it, rather than calling the guards, Popper instead tries to barter for the item, offering it to the party for 10,000 gold claiming it has "artisinalbite marks".

name: Fraygo's Flophouse
description: Upon approaching the building, any party member may pass aPerceptioncheck and recognize the flowery motif on the Flophouse patter, in case they have already found theFlowery Keyin the tunnels beneathOpen Hand TempleinRivington. Near the entrance,CrakandRiliaare discussing the lack of decent places to stay in the city.

name: Humbletoes
description: Vicar Humbletoesis the guardian of theStormshore Tabernacleand a merchant whom players can trade with.

name: Impress the Murder Tribunal
description: Impress the Murder Tribunalis a sub-quest of thequest⁠Get Orin's NetherstoneinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It is started automatically after enteringWyrm's Rock Fortressand long resting, or by entering the sewers for the first time.

name: Ifran
description: Ifran can be encountered near the entrance to theSteel Watch FoundryatX: -279 Y: -149. He carries aMotivatorand activates it in combat.

name: Get Orin's Netherstone
description: Get Orin's Netherstoneis aQuestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It is started automatically after enteringWyrm's Rock Fortressand long resting, or by entering the sewers for the first time.[1]

name: Lora's House
description: Lora's House[1]is a small two-storied building tucked in the corner between the Lower City Central Wall,Tefoco's HomeandCrimson Draughts.

name: Djinni
description: Djinn(singular: djinni) are a type of Elemental creature. They can be summoned to a spellcaster's aid with the spellPlanar Ally.

name: Highberry's Home
description: Highberry's Homeis a large building located directly north ofSorcerous Sundries. It has two spacious stories and a basement.

name: Gilded Chest
description: Gilded Chest has 50Hit Points.

name: Elerrathin's Home
description: Elerrathin's Home[1]is a large private building located to the south-west of theUpper City Gate Checkpoint. Together withThe LodgeandThe Glitter Galait lines up a small square with the statue ofBalduranin the middle.

name: Help the Devilish Ox
description: Help the Devilish Oxis aQuestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking with theStrange OxinRivington.

name: Sarin
description: Sarinis a dead, ghostly character located in theLower City Sewers.

name: Crimson Draughts
description: Crimson Draughtsis an apothecary, located just down the Lower City Central Wall, back-to-back toLavernica's Home, with the entrances atX: -81 Y: -95andX: -79 Y: -84, both leading into its first floor.

name: Allandra Grey
description: As the party approach the Water Queen's House, they hear a chorus of singing coming from within. Allandra and her priestesses are holding a funeral for the fallenHolli Dylford, who was blessed with the "divine mercy" of drowning.

name: Campsite
description: The campsite provides a location to swap or speak to party members, store or retrieve items from theTraveller's Chest, change class or revive fallenCompanionsviaWithers, or take aLong Rest. Through interacting with theMagic Mirror, Custom, Dark Urge, and Hireling characters (notOriginsorCompanions) can change their appearance. InMultiplayer, Withers' Wardrobe of Wayward Friends spawns near Withers when player characters are dismissed.

name: Silver Sword of the Astral Plane
description: ⁠Githyanki silver swordon theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Find Dribbles the Clown
description: Find Dribbles the Clownis aquestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withLucretious[1]in theCircus of the Last Days.

name: Sorcerous Sundries
description: Sorcerous Sundriesis a well-known store in Baldur's Gate, trading in various magical goods: elixirs and potions, spell scrolls, books, as well as weapons, armour and accessories.

name: Sarevok Anchev
description: Sarevok Anchevis a legendaryBhaalspawn. He was the main antagonist ofBaldur's Gateand a possible companion inBaldur's Gate II: Throne of Bhaal. He can be found in theMurder TribunalunderCandulhallow's TombstonesduringAct Three.

name: Lower City Sewers
description: The Lower City Sewers is a large expansive area covering much of theUndercityand also serving as the main access point into various nearby areas. The sewers can be accessed through numerous manhole covers on the surface, and connects to almost every other location of the Undercity except theMurder Tribunal.

name: Thrumbo
description: When meetingMystic Carrion, he believes that the party is too poor to seek his services. If asked if there is anything the party can do for him, Mystic Carrion then tasks the party with finding Thrumbo. This starts⁠Find Mystic Carrion's Servant.

name: Gloomy Fentonson
description: Gloomy Fentonson sells a variety of regular and enchanted armour as well as dyes.

name: Steel Watcher Titan
description: Steel Watcher Titanis a powerfulConstructwhich can be encountered in theSteel Watch FoundryduringAct Three.

name: Hahns Rives
description: Hahns Rives is the Black Gauntlet of the Foundry Lab Level in the Steel Watch Foundry. He is in charge of overseeing the Foundry's operations, and ordering and controlling theGondianworkers.[1]Hans was also in charge of sourcing a vessel and pilot,Redhammer, for transporting Gondian hostages to the Iron Throne.[2]Previously a research facility for theSteel Watchers, theIron Thronewas converted into a prison, mainly to contain the hostages.[3]

name: Mian
description: Iron Consul Mianis a cultist of⁠Bane.

name: Find Mystic Carrion's Servant
description: Find Mystic Carrion's Servantis aQuestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withMystic CarrioninPhilgrave's Mansion.

name: Bareki
description: Voiceless Penitent Barekiis a trader encountered inBaldur's Gate 3duringAct Three.

name: Indora Ralston
description: Ralston can be encountered in the Foundry Lab of theSteel Watch FoundryatX: -1971 Y: 445. She carries aMotivatorand activates it in combat.

name: Bhaal Temple
description: Bhaal Temple is the innermost sanctum of the cultists of⁠Bhaal, headed byOrin the Red. It is located deep within theUndercity, past theUndercity Ruins. Though the temple has been destroyed in the past, it was rebuilt and now serves as a home base for the cult of Bhaal.[2]

name: Campsite
description: The campsite provides a location to swap or speak to party members, store or retrieve items from theTraveller's Chest, change class or revive fallenCompanionsviaWithers, or take aLong Rest. Through interacting with theMagic Mirror, Custom, Dark Urge, and Hireling characters (notOriginsorCompanions) can change their appearance. InMultiplayer, Withers' Wardrobe of Wayward Friends spawns near Withers when player characters are dismissed.

name: Old Garlow's Place
description: Old Garlow's Placeis a house in the Heapside district of Baldur's Gate, standing on a rocky cliff on the shore of theRiver Chionthar. It has two stories, an attic and a basement.

name: Devil's Fee
description: Devil's Feeis a store specializing in all sorts of devilish artifacts and curios. It is located in the Heapside district of Baldur's Gate, ensconced between theForge of the Nineand the southern tower of theSzarr Palace, with the entrance just behind theBonecloak's Apothecary.

name: Luke
description: Strangler Lukeis a member of theCult of Bhaal. He joins the ambush on the party during the battle withFarslayer of Bhaal Ghislev.

name: Flail of Ages
description: ⁠Flail of Ageson theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Ancient Lair
description: The ancient lair serves as secret place secluded within theUndercity RuinsforMystic Carrionto work. It consists of a grand tomb as its main chamber and an attached workshop.

name: Volgu Drak
description: Volgu Drak can be found on the second floor ofFelogyr's Fireworks.

name: Ignur
description: Ignur can be encountered in the audience chamber of the Wyrm's Rock Fortress atX: 16 Y: 202afterGortash'scoronation.

name: Hyrriet
description: Fist of Bane Hyrrietis ahumanwho can be encountered inFelogyr's Fireworksworkshop in theLower Cityof theBaldur's Gate, inAct Threeof the game, while following through the quest⁠Investigate the Suspicious Toys.

name: Fist of Bane
description: Fists of Baneare the lowest rank of foot soldiers ofBanewho obey all orders without hesitation.

name: Bhaal
description:
Bhaalis thegodof murder, a member of theDead Threeand the progenitor of allBhaalspawn, who are his children. He is associated with the domain of Death. Bhaal cannot be the deity of aClericplayer character.
His worshippers in Baldur's Gate 3, theCult of Bhaal, also play a prominent role inAct Three.

name: Enver Gortash
description: LordEnver Gortash,[1][2]often just referred to asGortash, is the Chosen of⁠Bane, and one of the leaders of the enigmaticCult of the Absolute. He serves as one of the main antagonists inBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Guildhall
description: The Guildhall[1]is the underground headquarters ofThe Guild, the main criminal syndicate inBaldur’s Gate’sLower City. It is part of the largerUndercityarea. It serves as both a narrative hub for quests and a social space filled with shady characters, merchants, and story hooks.

name: Elfsong Tavern
description: TheElfsong Tavernis a well-known inn, pub and lodging house in the Eastway district of Baldur's Gate. It is located across theBaldur's Mouthpublishing house, and down the street from theStormshore Tabernacle.

name: Lord Jannath's Bodyguard
description: Lord Jannath's Bodyguard can be found at the mess hall, at the first floor of the Wyrm's Rock Fortress. She came with her master,Lord Raylen Jannath, and while waiting for her lord to descend from Audience Hall, spends time walking back and forth in the mess hall.

name: Garrett
description: Garrett was killed in the refugee camp inRivingtonafter he created a scene.

name: Mohlen
description: Mohlen can be encountered in the audience chamber of theWyrm's Rock FortressatX: 5 Y: 195afterGortash'scoronation.

name: Lady Jannath's Estate
description: Lady Jannath's Estate is a large manor house located in the Steeps district of theLower City, to the west of the main entrance into theUpper City- the Baldur's Gate checkpoint. It has four floors and a small garden along the facade.

name: Naaber
description: Upon entering the shop, Naaber tries to get the party's attention by hollering and running towards the party. If he is ignored, Naaber follows the party around[1]and continues to pester them until engaged. However, he stays inside the shop or nearby vicinity.

name: Water Elemental
description: Water Elementals can be created via the spellConjure Elemental. They can be found inSorcerous SundriesandThe Wyrmway.

name: Lucretious
description: Madam Lucretious,[1]also known as Bringer of the Night or Mama Lucretious, is ahigh elven[2]necromancer and the ringmistress of theCircus of the Last Days.

name: The Dragon's Sanctum
description: The Dragon's Sanctum is a place where, according to legends, the bronze wyrmAnsur, the protector of Baldur's Gate, waits in slumber for his chance to fulfill its oath,"answer the call and come to the city's aid".[1]The entrance into it is located at the far end ofThe Wyrmway.

name: Oskar Fevras
description: Oskar can be first found in theZhentarim Basement, where he is being held captive for ransom or sale byBremdue to his skill and renown as a painter. The party can attempt to purchase him as a slave, persuade Brem to release him, or kill all the Zhentarim in order to free him. This starts the questFree the Artist.

name: Firebolt?
description: ⁠Fire bolton theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Ulma
description: Ulmaand her fellow Gur can be found in the Gur Camp attending a funeral for the fallen from a recent assault on Cazador's palace. She leads the Gur, a group of monster hunters. Their children have been kidnapped byCazador'sspawns, and they have been camped in Rivington trying to mount a rescue or seek revenge. Ulma asks the party to help in defeating Cazador and discovering the fate of their abducted children.

name: Zanner Toobin
description: If not having travelled to theIron Throne, Zanner Toobin can be found in the back room of theSteel Watch Foundry. Tanner wears aKill Collar, which, in case of fighting against the Banites, must be disabled by deactivating aMotivatorheld by one of the Black Gauntlets. He informs them that theGondiansdare not fight back against the Banites for fear of killing the hostages in the Iron Throne. The party can either choose to heed his pleas and free the hostages first, or ignore them and turn all the Gondians hostile.

name: Knights of the Shield Hideout
description: The hideout is an abandoned meeting place for theKnights of the Shieldfrom where it operated in information dealing, political manipulation, and smuggling.

name: Ulder Ravengard
description: Ulder Ravengard is a recurring character in the Forgotten Realms setting ofDungeons and Dragons, appearing in the 5e adventures:⁠Murder in Baldur's Gate,⁠The Rise of Tiamat, and⁠Baldur's Gate: Descent into Avernus.

name: Avenge the Hag Survivors
description: Avenge the Hag Survivorsis aQuestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withMayrinaor the other hag survivors inOld Garlow's Place.

name: Gur Camp
description: TheGur Campis the temporary home to a group ofGurmonster hunters, led byUlma. It is located on the top of the hill atX: 102 Y: -12, north from theRequisitioned Barnand east of theAbandoned Windmill.

name: Mayrina
description: Mayrina, who is inside theRiverside Teahousewithin theSunlit Wetlands, seeks the help ofAuntie Ethelto resurrect her deceased husband Connor. She is the objective in⁠Save Mayrina.

name: That Which Watches
description: That Which Watchesis adeath knightguarding the entrance to the Hall of TheMurder Tribunal, located inBhaalist Crypt.

name: Visit the Emperor's Old Hideout
description: Visit the Emperor's Old Hideoutis aquestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by approaching theElfsong Tavern.

name: Uva
description: Uva can be found in the top office on the second floor ofFelogyr's FireworksatX: 2 Y: -115.

name: Dolor
description: Dolor[1]is a serial killer seeking to gain theBhaalisttitle ofUnholy Assassinby impressing theMurder Tribunal. He is the son ofFfion Goldgrind, who was a sex worker atSharess' Caressbefore being murdered by Dolor. He is first encountered under the false name MasterMeztli.

name: Philgrave's Mansion
description: Philgrave's Mansionis a large house located in the Brampton district of theLower City, to the south ofFelogyr's Fireworksand to the west from theGuild-controlled alleys. It has three floors and a basement.

name: Danthelon's Dancing Axe
description: Danthelon's Dancing Axe is an armour and weapon store located in a spacious three-storied building in the north-west ofWyrm's Crossing. It occupies ground and first floors of the building, and a vast cellar beneath the ground level. The cellar is actually a rendezvouz location for the Harpers, of which Danthelon is well aware, providing them not only the room, but cover as well.

name: Nuff
description: Iron Consul Nuffis a cultist ofBanefound in the audience chamber ofWyrm's Rock FortressatX: -10 Y: 212.

name: Mystic Carrion
description: Mystic Carrionis anundeadmummy lordinBaldur's Gate.

name: Mind flayer (windmill)
description: If a party member interacts with the mind flayer,The Emperorinforms them that it is a recently transformed mind flayer, and ushers them to kill it to consume its power. The party can sneak away via aStealthcheck or awaken the mind flayer.

name: Save the Gondians
description: Save the Gondiansis aquestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withZanner Toobinin theSteel Watch Foundry.

name: Jungle
description: This might be achieved by stealing hisDjinni Ringbefore playing his game, or (an option for aBardcharacter) by passing theDC 20PerceptionCheck(passive) and thenDC 15PersuasionCheckorDC 15PerformanceCheckto distract the djinni while actually playing the game. Only the party member speaking to Akabi will be teleported, and there is no way for other party members to travel there.

name: Dwarven Thrower
description: ⁠Dwarven throweron theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Rolan
description: Rolan can be first found in theEmerald Grovearguing withCalandLiaon whether they should remain in the Grove to help the tiefling refugees or leave. Rolan believes that they have no allegiance nor responsibility toward the refugees and should leave for Baldur's Gate for their own safety. Lia fervently argues that they should help the refugees because it is the right thing to do. She accuses him of being selfish, only caring about his apprenticeship toLorroakanat Baldur's Gate. The party can convince them to stay in the Grove or leave.[1]

name: Arms of Hadar
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Arms_of_Hadar_Visuals.mp4

name: Sticky Dondo
description: Dondo hawks his wares in the north-eastern corner of the main room, just before the bar. When pressed on where he sources his items, Dondo implies they were looted from the dead.

name: Aischa
description: Aischa can be found in the audience chamber of theWyrm's Rock FortressatX: 17 Y: 217afterGortash'scoronation.

name: Avenge the Drowned
description: Avenge the Drownedis aquestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withAllandra Greyin theLower City.

name: Forge of the Nine
description: TheForge of the Nineis a medium-sized building located in the Heapside district of Baldur's Gate, ensconced between theDevil's Feecurio shop, thegraveyard, and two private houses:Armbrust's HomeandHighberry's Home.

name: Wyrm's Rock Fortress
description: The Wyrm's Rock Fortress is standing on a rocky isle in the middle of the river Chionthar, taking up the whole space save for minor cliffs around. Wyrm's Rock Fortress is connected to the south toWyrm's Crossing, and to the north to theLower Cityby two drawbridges. As the legend goes, the isle once was home to abronze dragon, who swore to defend the city from greatest dangers.

name: Ferg Drogher
description: Ferg Drogheris ahumantraderencountered inAct Three. He is an opportunist looking to make gold off the crisis inRivington, buying heirlooms from desperate refugees in need of gold and selling their goods elsewhere with his shadowy supplier.

name: Lorroakan's Projection
description: Lorroakan's Projectionis a programmed facsimile ofLorroakanthat manages the front desk at theSorcerous Sundries.

name: Undercity
description: The Undercity is the seedy underbelly of the Lower City ofBaldur's Gatefeaturing the crumbling remains of ancient cities. It is filled with various shady characters, undead, and temples to evil deities or other undesirable elements that the city above (or the denizens themselves) would prefer to keep hidden from its citizens.

name: Steel Watch Foundry
description: The Steel Watch Foundry is a factory responsible for the fullSteel Watcherproduction cycle – one of the key elements inEnver Gortash'splan to seize and maintain control of the city of Baldur's Gate. The Steel Watch is not, however, entirely the invention of the Archduke nominee's. The prototype of a bipedal automaton was developed byGondiansin the⁠Hall of Wonders[1]in theUpper City, while Gortash refined the concept with an "Infernal Power Supply" and a method to control the Watchers remotely from a central hub.[2]

name: Orin
description: Orin the Redis the Chosen of⁠Bhaaland one of the leaders of the enigmaticCult of the Absoluteand serves as one of the main antagonists inBaldur's Gate 3.

name: Har'rak
description: Ch'r'ai Har'rakis aGithyankiPaladin[1]who can be encountered inKnights of the Shield HideoutinAct Three. She has been sent by⁠Vlaakithto learn more about the mysteriousEmperor.

name: Sharess' Caress
description: Sharess' Caress occupies a three-storied building on the stone bridge that contains theWyrm's Crossingitself. It has several lodgings atop the archway connecting the two sides of the street. It is named for⁠Sharess, the Faerûnian goddess of festhalls, hedonism, and sensual fulfillment.[1]

name: Ulder Ravengard
description: Ulder Ravengard is a recurring character in the Forgotten Realms setting ofDungeons and Dragons, appearing in the 5e adventures:⁠Murder in Baldur's Gate,⁠The Rise of Tiamat, and⁠Baldur's Gate: Descent into Avernus.

name: Hoots Hooligan
description: Hoots Hooligan sells a variety of alcoholic beverages. She tells the party that her most recent invention,Hoots' Hooch, can "beef them up" or "sharpen their tongue", or it could drop anyone in the blink of an eye. The party can choose to take their chances at which point Hoots will offer a taste for free, granting the following inspiration:

name: Chromatic Scale
description: Chromatic Scaleoccupies the upper floor of a building in the Steeps district of Baldur's Gate, adjacent to theFacemaker's Boutiqueand close to the bridge leading into theUpper Citythrough the Baldur's Gate checkpoint.

name: Circus of the Last Days
description: The Circus of the Last Days occupies the northwest corner of Rivington adjacent to theOpen Hand Temple. The circus is extraplanar, moving from plane to plane, and "thrilling all" who stop by. Currently it is visiting Rivington, and offers its patrons many forms of entertainment, including stage stand-ups, music, and various curios to purchase. The player character can also test their relationship with a romantic partner. Locals are familiar with the circus and its starring performerDribblesand have likely seen it before.

name: Open Hand Temple
description: The Open Hand Temple is a large shrine located south of theSouth Span Checkpointand across the road from theSword Coast Couriers.  The temple is dedicated to the worship of⁠Ilmater, and until recent events was a centre of healing, feeding and almsgiving for numerous waves of refugees fleeing the approaching armies of theAbsolute. Yet worship here, and aiding the bereaved, has been suspended due to the recent murder of thehead priest.

name: Entharl Danthelon
description: IfJaheirais recruited at the end ofAct Two, she informs the party of a Harper safehouse in Wyrm's Crossing, which is hidden in a shop calledDanthelon's Dancing Axe. The shop can be found on the west end of Wyrm's Crossing, across fromCarm's GarmsatX: -6 Y: 133. Upon entering, Entharl Danthelon greets the party. If Jaheira is present, she tells him to skip the pleasantries, and Entharl offers theRusty Key (Entharl)to the secret room atX: -18 Y: 138, where the Harpers await at the end of the iron ladder. If Jaheira is not present, the interacting party member can inform him that they were sent by Jaheira to meet some Harpers.

name: Dribbles
description: Dribbles the Clown, bornNorris Greenie,[text 1]is the "star" and one of the most popular shows at the Circus of the Last Days. Unfortunately, he is killed by cultists of⁠Bhaalsometime before the party reaches Baldur's Gate and replaced byFumm.

name: Lora Bergauz
description: Lora, also known asLora Bergauz,[2]is a human inBaldur's Gate, initially found duringAct Threeinside theBasilisk Gate Barracks. She is the mother ofVanra. Speaking to her can initiate the quest⁠Save Vanra.

name: Thomas C. Quirkilious
description: Thomas sells uniquely named musical instruments. These are functionally identical to the common musical instruments available throughout the game, though they cost significantly more.

name: Stormshore Armoury
description: The armoury stands atX: -38 Y: -83adjacent to the eastern side of theLower CityCentral Wall. It contains a forge with a large bellows, makeshift training dummies, and two stalls with armour and weapons respectively.

name: Echo of Abazigal
description: In life Abazigal was once a Bhaalspawn and a member of the Five, which was an alliance between five powerful Bhaalspawns formed by⁠Amelyssan, Bhaal's high priestess, that sought to slaughter their siblings in exchange for power. Like the rest of the Five, he was defeated by his sibling and fellow Bhaalspawn "Gorion's Ward" and their party at his lair.

name: Water Queen's House
description: The Water Queen's House is a temple dedicated to the deityQueen Umberlee. It lies south of theGrey Harbour Docksand only has one way of entering. The temple consists of a large portico in the front used for offering and worship by commoners, and the temple proper in the back which is being used by priestesses of Umberlee.

name: Requisitioned Barn
description: The Requisitioned Barn is located atX: 83 Y: -108. It is currently in use as a storehouse for donations collected to be distributed among the refugees.

name: Sword of Chaos
description: ⁠Sword of Chaoson theForgotten Realms Wiki

name: Highberry's Home
description: Highberry's Homeis a large building located directly north ofSorcerous Sundries. It has two spacious stories and a basement.

name: Felogyr's Fireworks
description: Felogyr's Fireworksshop is located in Heapside district of the Baldur's Gate. The main entrance is atX: 13 Y: -127.

name: Akabi
description: Akabiis adjinniwho can be encountered inRivingtonduringAct ThreeofBaldur's Gate 3. He is a performer at theCircus of the Last DaysinRivington, located atX: -68 Y: -75where he can be found hosting a Spin the Wheel game.

name: Profane Scourge
description: https://bg3.wiki/wiki/File:Profane_Scourge_Action.mp4

name: Necromancy
description: Necromancyis a school ofmagic.

name: Abandoned Windmill
description: The windmill can be found up the hill atX: 44 Y: -32, north from theRequisitioned Barnand west of theGur Camp. A blood trail leads to its entrance. When the party first approaches the windmill, they hear noises of distress coming from the cellar.

name: Voss
description: Kith'rak Vossis a ruthlessGithyankiknight in the service of his queen,⁠Vlaakith. He rides a red dragon namedQudenos.

name: Save Vanra
description: Save Vanrais aQuestinAct Threeof Baldur's Gate 3. It can be started by speaking withLora Bergauzin theLower City. This quest is only available if⁠Save Mayrinawas completed in Act One.

name: Ansur
description: Ansuris anundead dragonfound inBaldur's Gate 3. Formerly abronze dragonnicknamed "The Waiting Storm" and the "Heart of the Gate", Ansur was generally thought to be mere legend. Depending on the party's actions, eitherUlder Ravengard,Counsellor Florrick, orCordula Eltanreveals that Ansur is very real, offering the party a book which starts their journey to seek Ansur and its help against theAbsolute.

name: Fireliia Jannath
description: Lady Jannath is the fiancé ofOskar Fevras. She can be found ather estatein theLower CityofBaldur's Gate. If the party rescued Oskar Fevras during⁠Free the ArtistinAct One, he is possessed by a ghost in her bedroom, taunting Lady Jannath and claiming that he only married her for her gold. ThreePoltergeiststhen appear and engage the party in combat. Once they are dealt with, Oskar falls unconscious and Jannath recognizes the party as Oskar's saviors from paintings he has made of them. She begs for help and explains that he has not been acting like himself since the wedding; she suspects that it has something to do with his atelier since that is the place where he spent most of his time.

name: Severn
description: He can be found in the Guildhall duringAct 3, where he sells various alcoholic beverages and drugs. While some drugs can be found in areas around Baldur's Gate or looted off the corpses, Severn is the only trader that regularly sells and restocks them.
